levels.name: A1 — Beginner
levels.description: Basic greetings, introductions, and survival English.

days.grammar: Present tense of 'to be'

examples.en: Hello! How are you?
examples.es: ¡Hola! ¿Cómo estás?

examples.en: Good morning, I am John.
examples.es: Buenos días, soy John.

examples.en: Goodbye, see you soon!
examples.es: ¡Adiós, nos vemos pronto!

days.tip: English greetings don't change by gender like in Spanish. 'Hello' is neutral for all situations.

quiz.q: What do you say when you leave?
Translate: Good morning
What means 'Thanks'?

days.grammar: Subject pronouns (I, you, he, she, it, we, they)

examples.en: I am a student. You are a teacher.
examples.es: Soy estudiante. Eres profesor.

examples.en: He is from Spain. She is from Mexico.
examples.es: Él es de España. Ella es de México.

examples.en: They are friends.
examples.es: Son amigos.

days.tip: English pronouns don't have gender variations like Spanish tú/vosotros/ustedes. 'You' is used for everyone!

quiz.q: Which is the plural 'you'?
Translate: Ella es ingeniera
What pronoun means 'nosotros'?

days.grammar: Present simple: I am, you are, he/she/it is, we/they are

examples.en: I am happy.
examples.es: Estoy feliz.

examples.en: You are a teacher.
examples.es: Eres profesor.

examples.en: She is a nurse.
examples.es: Es enfermera.

days.tip: Spanish uses 'ser' and 'estar', but English only has 'be'. 'I am' = 'soy/estoy', so context matters!

quiz.q: What is the correct form? 'I ___ a student.'
He ___ a doctor.
They ___ happy.

days.grammar: Cardinal numbers and basic counting

examples.en: I am twenty years old.
examples.es: Tengo veinte años.

examples.en: The phone number is: one, two, three, four.
examples.es: El número telefónico es: uno, dos, tres, cuatro.

examples.en: There are five people here.
examples.es: Hay cinco personas aquí.

days.tip: In Spanish you say 'tengo veinte años' (I have twenty years), but in English you say 'I am twenty years old'. Different structure!

quiz.q: How do you count to 5?
Write the number: fifteen
How many? 'I have three books'

days.grammar: Review: present tense of 'to be', pronouns, numbers

examples.en: Hello, I am Maria. I am a teacher. I am from Spain.
examples.es: Hola, soy María. Soy profesora. Soy de España.

examples.en: Hi, you are a student. There are twenty students.
examples.es: Hola, eres estudiante. Hay veinte estudiantes.

examples.en: Goodbye! I am happy. See you!
examples.es: ¡Adiós! Estoy feliz. ¡Nos vemos!

days.tip: Remember: 'I am' (singular), 'we are' (plural). Don't mix them up!

flashcards.front: My name is John. ¿Cuál es el pronombre?
Hello, goodbye, hi, bye. ¿Saludos o despedidas?

quiz.q: Complete: 'I ___ happy.'
How do you say 'Él es profesor'?
What number is 'fifteen'?

days.grammar: Possessive adjectives (my, your, his, her)

examples.en: My mother is a doctor.
examples.es: Mi madre es doctora.

examples.en: His sister is beautiful.
examples.es: Su hermana es hermosa.

examples.en: Her family is large.
examples.es: Su familia es grande.

days.tip: Spanish has one word for 'his/her' (su), but English distinguishes them. 'His' = de él, 'her' = de ella!

quiz.q: 'My mother is...' - which is correct?
His brother - in Spanish?
Her family is nice. Which possessive?

days.grammar: Color adjectives and 'to be'

examples.en: The car is red.
examples.es: El coche es rojo.

examples.en: My house is white.
examples.es: Mi casa es blanca.

examples.en: The sky is blue.
examples.es: El cielo es azul.

days.tip: In Spanish, colors agree with gender (rojo/roja), but in English colors NEVER change! 'Red car' not 'reds car'.

quiz.q: The apple is...
Mi casa es azul. In English?
Which is orange?

days.grammar: Nationality adjectives

examples.en: I am from Spain. I am Spanish.
examples.es: Soy de España. Soy español.

examples.en: She is Mexican. She is from Mexico.
examples.es: Ella es mexicana. Es de México.

examples.en: They are from England. They are English.
examples.es: Son de Inglaterra. Son ingleses.

days.tip: In Spanish you say 'soy de España' but in English we often just say 'I am Spanish' or 'I am from Spain'. Both work!

quiz.q: I am from France. I am...
¿Cuál es la nacionalidad de China?
They are from Spain. They are...

days.grammar: Present simple with daily verbs

examples.en: I wake up at 7 AM.
examples.es: Me despierto a las 7 AM.

examples.en: He goes to work at 8 AM.
examples.es: Él va al trabajo a las 8 AM.

examples.en: She has dinner at 7 PM.
examples.es: Ella tiene cena a las 7 PM.

days.tip: 'Have breakfast/lunch/dinner' sounds strange to Spanish speakers who say 'desayunar', but it's the natural way to say it in English!

quiz.q: I ___ at 7 AM. (despertarse)
He ___ to work. (ir)
She ___ at 7 PM. (comer)

days.grammar: Review: possessive adjectives, colors, nationalities

examples.en: My family is from Spain. My mother is Spanish. She has blue eyes. My father is a doctor.
examples.es: Mi familia es de España. Mi madre es española. Tiene ojos azules. Mi padre es médico.

examples.en: His sister is Mexican. Her car is red. They are from Mexico.
examples.es: Su hermana es mexicana. Su coche es rojo. Son de México.

examples.en: Your brother is a teacher. His house is white and green.
examples.es: Tu hermano es profesor. Su casa es blanca y verde.

days.tip: 'Have blue eyes' = 'tener ojos azules'. Spanish speakers often forget the 'have' here!

flashcards.front: Mi madre es doctora. Pronombre posesivo?

quiz.q: My ___ is from China. (hermana)
The flag is red and ___. (blanco)
I am ___ from France. (francesa)

days.grammar: Count and uncountable nouns with 'a' and 'some'

examples.en: I have a coffee.
examples.es: Tengo un café.

examples.en: She wants some bread.
examples.es: Ella quiere pan.

examples.en: Do you like apples?
examples.es: ¿Te gustan las manzanas?

days.tip: 'Some milk' (uncountable) vs 'some apples' (countable). Spanish doesn't always distinguish, but English does!

quiz.q: I have ___ coffee. (un)
Do you like ___? (verduras)
She wants ___ bread. (pan)

days.grammar: Present simple questions and polite requests

examples.en: Can I have a table for two?
examples.es: ¿Puedo tener una mesa para dos?

examples.en: I want a pizza, please.
examples.es: Quiero una pizza, por favor.

examples.en: The food is delicious!
examples.es: ¡La comida es deliciosa!

days.tip: Always say 'please' and 'thank you' in English! It's more important than in Spanish culture.

quiz.q: 'Can I have a table for two?' What are you asking?
I ___ a pizza. (quiero)
The food is ___. (delicioso)

days.grammar: Prepositions of place (in, on, at, near, next to)

examples.en: The restaurant is near the park.
examples.es: El restaurante está cerca del parque.

examples.en: Turn left at the corner.
examples.es: Gira a la izquierda en la esquina.

examples.en: It is on Main Street.
examples.es: Está en la Calle Mayor.

days.tip: English has specific prepositions: 'on the street', 'at the corner', 'in the city'. Spanish is more flexible, so learn these phrases!

quiz.q: Turn ___ at the corner. (izquierda)
The school is ___ the bank. (cerca de)
It is ___ Main Street. (en la calle)

days.grammar: Present simple for shopping transactions

examples.en: How much is this shirt?
examples.es: ¿Cuánto cuesta esta camisa?

examples.en: It is too expensive.
examples.es: Es demasiado caro.

examples.en: Do you have it in blue?
examples.es: ¿Lo tienes en azul?

days.tip: 'How much is it?' = 'How much does it cost?' Both work! Spanish would say 'Cuánto cuesta' literally.

quiz.q: 'How much is this?' you want to know the...
This shirt is very ___. (caro)
Do you have it in ___? (azul)

days.grammar: Review: countable/uncountable, prepositions, imperatives

examples.en: I want some bread and a coffee. The restaurant is near the park. Turn left.
examples.es: Quiero pan y un café. El restaurante está cerca del parque. Gira a la izquierda.

examples.en: This shirt is expensive. Do you have it in white? It is too much money.
examples.es: Esta camisa es cara. ¿La tienes en blanco? Es demasiado dinero.

examples.en: The waiter brings the menu. I order chicken. The food is delicious!
examples.es: El camarero trae el menú. Pido pollo. ¡La comida es deliciosa!

days.tip: Review: 'some' for uncountable (some bread), 'a/an' for countable (a coffee). Don't forget!

flashcards.front: Quiero un café y pan. ¿A o some?
flashcards.back: A for coffee, some for bread

quiz.q: I want ___ coffee and ___ bread.
Where is the restaurant? It is ___ the park.
This is ___! (delicioso)

days.grammar: Time expressions and 'What time is it?'

examples.en: What time is it? It is 3 o'clock.
examples.es: ¿Qué hora es? Son las 3.

examples.en: It is 10:30 (half past ten).
examples.es: Son las diez y media.

examples.en: The meeting is at 2 PM.
examples.es: La reunión es a las 2 PM.

days.tip: English says 'o'clock' which Spanish doesn't use. Also: 'half past' (y media), 'quarter past' (y cuarto), 'quarter to' (menos cuarto).

quiz.q: It is 3:00. You say...
Half past ten is...
What time? 2 PM en español?

days.grammar: Days and frequency expressions

examples.en: Today is Monday. Yesterday was Sunday.
examples.es: Hoy es lunes. Ayer fue domingo.

examples.en: I work on weekdays.
examples.es: Trabajo entre semana.

examples.en: What day is it? It is Friday.
examples.es: ¿Qué día es? Es viernes.

days.tip: In English, days of the week are ALWAYS capitalized. In Spanish they're lowercase! Remember: Monday, not monday.

quiz.q: Today is ___ (capitalize!)
Ayer fue domingo. Yesterday was...
¿Cuántos días hay en una semana?

days.grammar: Month names and date expressions

examples.en: My birthday is in March.
examples.es: Mi cumpleaños es en marzo.

examples.en: The meeting is on January 15th.
examples.es: La reunión es el 15 de enero.

examples.en: Winter is from December to February.
examples.es: El invierno es de diciembre a febrero.

days.tip: English months are CAPITALIZED (January), Spanish are lowercase (enero). Also: 'in March' but 'on March 15th'!

quiz.q: My birthday is ___ May. (preposición)
The date is ___ March 20th.
¿Cuántos meses hay en invierno?

days.grammar: Weather expressions with 'it is'

examples.en: It is sunny today.
examples.es: Hace sol hoy.

examples.en: What is the weather like? It is cold.
examples.es: ¿Qué tiempo hace? Hace frío.

examples.en: It is raining.
examples.es: Está lloviendo.

days.tip: Spanish says 'Hace calor' (it makes heat), but English says 'It is hot'. The structure is completely different!

quiz.q: What is the weather like? ___
Hace frío. In English?
It is ___. (lloviendo)

days.grammar: Review: time expressions, prepositions in/on

examples.en: Today is Monday, March 15th. It is 3 o'clock and it is sunny. My birthday is in March.
examples.es: Hoy es lunes, 15 de marzo. Son las 3 y hace sol. Mi cumpleaños es en marzo.

examples.en: What time is the meeting? It is at 10 o'clock on Friday. The weather is cold.
examples.es: ¿A qué hora es la reunión? Es a las 10 el viernes. Hace frío.

examples.en: Yesterday was Sunday. Tomorrow is Tuesday. The temperature is warm.
examples.es: Ayer fue domingo. Mañana es martes. La temperatura es templada.

days.tip: Review: 'on' for specific days/dates, 'in' for months/seasons. 'What time' vs 'What day' - different!

quiz.q: It is 3:30. Half past three or quarter past?
Today is Monday, January 5th. What prepositions?
The weather is ___. (hace frío)

days.grammar: There is / There are + prepositions

examples.en: There is a table in the kitchen.
examples.es: Hay una mesa en la cocina.

examples.en: There are three bedrooms in my house.
examples.es: Hay tres dormitorios en mi casa.

examples.en: The sofa is in the living room.
examples.es: El sofá está en la sala.

days.tip: 'There is' (singular) vs 'There are' (plural). In Spanish just 'Hay' for both, but English distinguishes!

quiz.q: There ___ a table in the kitchen.
There ___ three bedrooms.
En inglés, ¿dónde está la silla?
options: The chair is in living room
In the chair is living room

days.grammar: 'What do you do?' + occupations

examples.en: What do you do? I am a teacher.
examples.es: ¿Cuál es tu trabajo? Soy profesor.

examples.en: She is an engineer.
examples.es: Ella es ingeniera.

examples.en: They are doctors.
examples.es: Son doctores.

days.tip: Notice: 'a teacher' (singular), 'teachers' (plural). In Spanish: profesor/profesora. English doesn't have gender in job titles!

quiz.q: What do you do? ___ a teacher.
She is ___ engineer. (artículo)
¿Cuál es el plural de 'doctor'?

days.grammar: Prepositions with transport modes

examples.en: I go to work by car.
examples.es: Voy al trabajo en coche.

examples.en: She travels by train.
examples.es: Ella viaja en tren.

examples.en: Do you walk or take the bus?
examples.es: ¿Caminas o coges el autobús?

days.tip: 'By car' (en coche), 'by train' (en tren), 'on foot' (a pie). English uses 'by' for most, but 'on' for public transport!

quiz.q: I go to work ___ car.
She ___ the train. (coge)
Do you walk ___ ride a bike?

days.grammar: Questions with do/does (third person)

examples.en: Do you work here? Yes, I do.
examples.es: ¿Trabajas aquí? Sí.

examples.en: Does he like coffee? No, he doesn't.
examples.es: ¿Le gusta el café? No.

examples.en: What do they do? They are engineers.
examples.es: ¿Qué hacen? Son ingenieros.

days.tip: 'Do I/you/we/they work?' vs 'Does he/she/it work?' Spanish just changes the verb form, but English adds 'does'!

quiz.q: ___ you work here?
___ she like coffee?
Yes, I ___. (sí, trabajo)

days.grammar: Review: there is/are, do/does, by/on

examples.en: There are three bedrooms in my house. I am a teacher. I go to work by car.
examples.es: Hay tres dormitorios en mi casa. Soy profesor. Voy al trabajo en coche.

examples.en: Does he work here? Yes, he does. There is a table in the kitchen. What do you do?
examples.es: ¿Trabaja aquí? Sí. Hay una mesa en la cocina. ¿Cuál es tu trabajo?

examples.en: There is a living room. She is an engineer. They travel by train.
examples.es: Hay una sala. Ella es ingeniera. Viajan en tren.

days.tip: 'There is' singular, 'there are' plural. 'Do' for most, 'Does' for he/she/it. Remember!

flashcards.front: ¿Cuál es tu trabajo? Soy profesor. ¿What do you do?
Hay una mesa y cuatro sillas. ¿Is/are?
flashcards.back: There is one table and there are four chairs

flashcards.front: ¿Cogen el autobús? Do/Does? Do they take the bus? ¿Respuesta? Yes, they do.

quiz.q: There ___ five rooms in my house.
What ___ you do? I am an engineer.
I go to work ___ bus.

days.grammar: 'Was' and 'were' in past

examples.en: I was at home yesterday.
examples.es: Estaba en casa ayer.

examples.en: She was a student. Now she is a teacher.
examples.es: Era estudiante. Ahora es profesora.

examples.en: They were happy.
examples.es: Fueron felices.

days.tip: 'I was', 'he/she/it was', but 'we/you/they were'. Spanish just has 'era' or 'estaba' but English has was/were!

quiz.q: I ___ at home yesterday.
They ___ students.
She ___ a teacher. Now she is a lawyer.

days.grammar: Past tense verbs ending in -ed

examples.en: I walked to school yesterday.
examples.es: Caminé a la escuela ayer.

examples.en: She studied English last week.
examples.es: Estudió inglés la semana pasada.

examples.en: They played football.
examples.es: Jugaron fútbol.

days.tip: In Spanish, you change the verb ending (caminé, estudié), and in English you add 'd' or 'ed' (walked, studied). The structure is similar but different!

quiz.q: I ___ English yesterday. (estudié)
She ___ to school. (caminó)
They ___ football. (jugaron)

days.grammar: Irregular verbs (go, eat, see, have, etc.)

examples.en: I went to the park yesterday.
examples.es: Fui al parque ayer.

examples.en: She ate an apple.
examples.es: Comió una manzana.

examples.en: They saw a movie.
examples.es: Vieron una película.

days.tip: Irregular verbs don't follow the -ed pattern. You have to memorize them! 'Go → went' (not 'goed'). Spanish also has irregular verbs (ir → fui).

quiz.q: I ___ to the park yesterday. (fui)
She ___ an apple. (comió)
They ___ a movie. (vieron)

days.grammar: 'Did not' and questions with 'did'

examples.en: I didn't go to school yesterday.
examples.es: No fui a la escuela ayer.

examples.en: Did you study? Yes, I did.
examples.es: ¿Estudiaste? Sí.

examples.en: What did she do? She went to work.
examples.es: ¿Qué hizo? Fue al trabajo.

days.tip: 'Didn't' + base verb (didn't go, didn't eat). Spanish changes the verb (no fui), but English uses 'didn't' + infinitive!

quiz.q: I ___ go to school yesterday.
___ you study? Yes, I ___. (pregunta y respuesta)
What ___ she do? (¿Qué hizo?)

days.grammar: Review: was/were, -ed endings, irregular verbs, did/didn't

examples.en: Yesterday I went to school. I studied English. I didn't eat lunch. I was happy.
examples.es: Ayer fui a la escuela. Estudié inglés. No comí almuerzo. Estaba feliz.

examples.en: Did she go to work? Yes, she did. She worked all day. She ate dinner.
examples.es: ¿Fue al trabajo? Sí. Trabajó todo el día. Comió cena.

examples.en: They didn't see the movie. They went home. They were tired.
examples.es: No vieron la película. Fueron a casa. Estaban cansados.

days.tip: Review all three: was/were (I was, he was, they were), regular -ed (walked, studied), irregular (went, ate, saw). Practice!

quiz.q: I ___ at home yesterday.
She ___ an apple.
Did you go? No, I ___.

days.grammar: 'Going to' for future plans

examples.en: I am going to travel tomorrow.
examples.es: Voy a viajar mañana.

examples.en: She is going to visit her family next week.
examples.es: Va a visitar a su familia la próxima semana.

examples.en: They are going to eat dinner at 7.
examples.es: Van a cenar a las 7.

days.tip: 'Going to' = 'going' + 'to' + base verb. In Spanish: 'voy a' + infinitive. Same structure!

flashcards.back: I am going to travel tomorrow.
What are you going to do?

quiz.q: I ___ going to travel tomorrow. (complement)
She ___ going to study English.
What ___ you going to do?

days.grammar: 'Will' for future decisions

examples.en: I will call you tomorrow.
examples.es: Te llamaré mañana.

examples.en: She will probably come.
examples.es: Probablemente vendrá.

examples.en: They won't forget.
examples.es: No olvidarán.

days.tip: 'Will' + base verb (I will go, not 'I will going'). Spanish uses future tense (iré), but English uses 'will'.

quiz.q: I ___ call you. (llamaré)
She ___ come. (negative)
What ___ you do tomorrow?

days.grammar: Comparative adjectives (bigger, more beautiful)

examples.en: This house is bigger than that one.
examples.es: Esta casa es más grande que esa.

examples.en: She is taller than him.
examples.es: Ella es más alta que él.

examples.en: My car is as fast as yours.
examples.es: Mi coche es tan rápido como el tuyo.

days.tip: Short adjectives: add -er (bigger). Long adjectives: use 'more' (more beautiful). Spanish just uses 'más + adjective'.

quiz.q: This house is ___ than that one. (más grande)
She is ___ than him. (más alta)
My car is ___ yours. (tan rápido como)

days.grammar: Superlative adjectives (the biggest, the most beautiful)

examples.en: He is the tallest in the class.
examples.es: Es el más alto de la clase.

examples.en: That is the most beautiful house.
examples.es: Esa es la casa más hermosa.

examples.en: It is the best restaurant in the city.
examples.es: Es el mejor restaurante de la ciudad.

days.tip: 'The' + comparative/superlative (the tallest). Spanish: 'el más alto'. Remember 'the' in English!

quiz.q: He is ___ tallest in the class.
That is ___ most beautiful house. (traducción)
Best = superlativo de ___

days.grammar: Review: going to, will, -er/-est, more/most

examples.en: I am going to travel tomorrow. My car is bigger than yours. It is the fastest car in the city.
examples.es: Voy a viajar mañana. Mi coche es más grande que el tuyo. Es el coche más rápido de la ciudad.

examples.en: She will study. She is more intelligent than him. She is the most intelligent in the class.
examples.es: Ella estudiará. Ella es más inteligente que él. Es la más inteligente de la clase.

examples.en: They won't forget. This restaurant is more expensive than that one. It is the best restaurant.
examples.es: No olvidarán. Este restaurante es más caro que ese. Es el mejor restaurante.

days.tip: Future: 'going to' for plans, 'will' for decisions. Comparatives: -er or 'more'. Superlatives: 'the' + -est or 'the most'.

flashcards.front: Mi casa es tan grande como la tuya. ¿Estructura?

quiz.q: I ___ going to travel tomorrow.
This is ___ than that. (más grande)
He is ___ strongest in the team.

days.grammar: All A1 grammar tenses

examples.en: Hello! I am John. I am a teacher from Spain. My family is large. I work at a school.
examples.es: ¡Hola! Soy John. Soy profesor de España. Mi familia es grande. Trabajo en una escuela.

examples.en: This house is bigger than that one. It has three bedrooms. The weather is sunny. I will travel tomorrow.
examples.es: Esta casa es más grande que esa. Tiene tres dormitorios. Hace sol. Viajaré mañana.

examples.en: Yesterday I studied English. I am going to read a book. The best restaurant is near my home.
examples.es: Ayer estudié inglés. Voy a leer un libro. El mejor restaurante está cerca de mi casa.

days.tip: Review all vocabulary from A1! Create sentences combining multiple topics.

flashcards.front: Temas A1: Saludos, familia, comida, trabajo, tiempo, transporte, números
flashcards.back: Greetings, family, food, jobs, time, transport, numbers
To be, present, past, future tenses

flashcards.front: Pronombres: yo, tú, él, ella, nosotros, ellos
flashcards.back: I, you, he, she, we, they

flashcards.front: Adjetivos: grande, pequeño, hermoso, alto, caro, barato
flashcards.back: Big, small, beautiful, tall, expensive, cheap

flashcards.front: Preposiciones: in, on, at, near, by, from, to
flashcards.back: En, sobre, en, cerca, por, de, a

quiz.q: What is your name? You answer...
How do you ask 'How are you?' in formal?
What do you say goodbye?

days.grammar: Spanish-English differences and common errors

examples.en: I am excited! (not: I am excitado!)
examples.es: ¡Estoy emocionado! (no: 'estoy excitado')

examples.en: My parents are here. (not: My progenitors)
examples.es: Mis padres están aquí. (no: 'mis progenitores')

examples.en: The library is on the left. (not: The book shop)
examples.es: La biblioteca está a la izquierda. (no: 'la librería = bookstore')

days.tip: False friends are words that look similar but have different meanings! 'Embarrassed' ≠ 'Embarazada' (pregnant). Learn them well!

flashcards.front: Excitado (padre) en español = Excited en inglés
Librería (bookstore) en español ≠ Library en inglés (biblioteca)
Carpeta (folder) en español ≠ Carpet en inglés (alfombra)
Éxito (success) en español ≠ Exit en inglés (salida)
Actual (current) en español ≠ Actual en inglés (real/current)
Constipado (cold) en español ≠ Constipated en inglés (estreñido)
flashcards.back: Constipated = estreñido, constipado = having a cold

quiz.q: I'm pregnant in Spanish, but in English I say...
Where is the library? (¿Dónde está la biblioteca?)
The carpet is red. ¿Carpeta en este contexto?

days.grammar: Practical conversation phrases

examples.en: Excuse me, can you help me? Where is the bathroom? Thank you very much!
examples.es: Disculpa, ¿puedes ayudarme? ¿Dónde está el baño? ¡Muchas gracias!

examples.en: Good morning. I would like a coffee, please. How much is it? Thank you!
examples.es: Buenos días. Me gustaría un café, por favor. ¿Cuánto cuesta? ¡Gracias!

examples.en: Hi! What time is the meeting? It is at 3 o'clock. See you then!
examples.es: ¡Hola! ¿A qué hora es la reunión? Es a las 3. ¡Nos vemos!

days.tip: In real conversations, people are VERY polite in English. Always use 'please', 'thank you', 'excuse me', 'sorry'!

quiz.q: You want to ask for help. You say...
Someone helps you. You say...
You made a mistake. You say...

days.grammar: Review of all A1 grammar

examples.en: I am a teacher. Yesterday I worked. Tomorrow I will teach. Next week I am going to visit Spain.
examples.es: Soy profesor. Ayer trabajé. Mañana enseñaré. La próxima semana voy a visitar España.

examples.en: She was at home. She studied. She didn't go out. She is going to sleep.
examples.es: Estaba en casa. Estudió. No salió. Va a dormir.

examples.en: They are friends. They were at the party. They went home. They will see each other tomorrow.
examples.es: Son amigos. Estaban en la fiesta. Se fueron a casa. Se verán mañana.

days.tip: A1 covers: 'to be' (present/past), present simple, past simple, future 'going to' and 'will'. Practice switching between them!

flashcards.front: Presente: I am, you are, he is. ¿Estructura?
Pasado: I was, he was, they were. ¿Estructura?
Pasado regular: I worked, she studied. ¿Estructura?
Pasado irregular: I went, she ate. ¿Estructura?
Futuro 1: I am going to work. ¿Estructura?

quiz.q: Completa: I ___ a teacher. Yesterday I ___ a student.
She ___ coffee yesterday. She doesn't ___ coffee now.
They ___ going to travel tomorrow.

days.grammar: All A1 concepts summary

examples.en: Congratulations! You finished A1! You can now introduce yourself, talk about family, order food, and ask for directions.
examples.es: ¡Felicitaciones! ¡Terminaste A1! Ahora puedes presentarte, hablar de familia, pedir comida y preguntar direcciones.

examples.en: You learned 400+ words, 8 weeks of lessons, and practiced grammar every day. Well done! Next is A2!
examples.es: Aprendiste 400+ palabras, 8 semanas de lecciones y practicaste gramática todos los días. ¡Bien hecho! ¡Lo siguiente es A2!

examples.en: You are ready for A2 level. You will learn present continuous, past perfect, and more conversations!
examples.es: Estás listo para el nivel A2. Aprenderás presente continuo, pasado perfecto y más conversaciones.

days.tip: Congratulations on completing A1! You now have a solid foundation. Keep practicing and move to A2!

flashcards.front: Aprendiste saludos, familia, comida, trabajo, tiempo, transporte
flashcards.back: You learned greetings, family, food, jobs, time, transport
Now you are ready for A2

quiz.q: You finished A1! Next level is...
In A1, how many weeks of lessons did you complete?
What did you learn in A1?

levels.name: A2 — Elementary
levels.description: Building on basics: present continuous, more past tense, advice, and conversational fluency.

days.grammar: 'Am/is/are' + '-ing'

examples.en: I am reading a book right now.
examples.es: Estoy leyendo un libro ahora.

examples.en: She is eating lunch.
examples.es: Ella está comiendo almuerzo.

examples.en: They are playing football.
examples.es: Están jugando fútbol.

days.tip: Present continuous: verb + 'ing' (eating, reading). Spanish: estar + gerundio. Same concept, different form!

quiz.q: I ___ a book right now. (estoy leyendo)
She ___ lunch.
What ___ you ___? (¿Qué estás haciendo?)

days.grammar: Negative and questions with present continuous

examples.en: I am not working today.
examples.es: No estoy trabajando hoy.

examples.en: Is she eating? No, she isn't.
examples.es: ¿Está comiendo? No.

examples.en: What are they doing? They are playing.
examples.es: ¿Qué están haciendo? Están jugando.

days.tip: Negatives: 'am not', 'is not', 'are not'. Questions: 'Am I...?', 'Is he...?', 'Are they...?'

quiz.q: I ___ working today.
___ she eating?
What ___ they ___?

days.grammar: Difference between simple and continuous

examples.en: I usually work in an office. Right now I am working from home.
examples.es: Usualmente trabajo en una oficina. Ahora estoy trabajando desde casa.

examples.en: She eats lunch at 12. She is eating lunch now.
examples.es: Ella come almuerzo a las 12. Ella está comiendo almuerzo ahora.

examples.en: They play football every week. They are playing now.
examples.es: Juegan fútbol cada semana. Están jugando ahora.

days.tip: Simple = habit or general truth. Continuous = right now, temporary action. Spanish distinction is same!

quiz.q: I ___ work in an office. I ___ from home today.
She ___ lunch at 12. She ___ lunch now.
What's the difference entre simple y continuous?

days.grammar: Adding '-ing' to verbs (no silent 'e', double consonant)

examples.en: Make → making (drop the 'e')
examples.es: Hacer → haciendo (drop la 'e')

examples.en: Run → running (double the consonant)
examples.es: Correr → corriendo (dobla la consonante)

examples.en: Eat → eating (just add '-ing')
examples.es: Comer → comiendo (solo añade '-ing')

days.tip: Rules: (1) Drop silent 'e': make → making. (2) Double consonant: run → running. (3) Just add: eat → eating.

quiz.q: Make + ing = ?
Run + ing = ?
Write the -ing form of 'sit'

days.grammar: Review: present continuous form, negatives, vs simple

examples.en: I am studying English right now. She is eating. They are not working today.
examples.es: Estoy estudiando inglés ahora. Ella está comiendo. No están trabajando hoy.

examples.en: What are you doing? Are they playing? Yes, they are.
examples.es: ¿Qué estás haciendo? ¿Están jugando? Sí.

examples.en: I usually work here. I am working from home today. She eats lunch at 12. She is eating now.
examples.es: Usualmente trabajo aquí. Estoy trabajando desde casa hoy. Come almuerzo a las 12. Está comiendo ahora.

days.tip: Present continuous = am/is/are + verb-ing. For now, temporary, activities happening.

flashcards.front: Como almuerzo a las 12, pero ahora estoy comiendo. ¿Qué diferencia?

quiz.q: Right now I ___ a book.
Are you working? ___
I usually work here. I ___ from home today.

days.grammar: Always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never

examples.en: I always drink coffee in the morning.
examples.es: Siempre bebo café por la mañana.

examples.en: She usually goes to the gym.
examples.es: Ella usualmente va al gimnasio.

examples.en: They never eat vegetables.
examples.es: Nunca comen verduras.

days.tip: Position: before main verb (always drink) or after 'to be' (I am always). In Spanish just 'siempre' but English word order matters!

quiz.q: I ___ drink coffee. (siempre)
She ___ late for work. (nunca)
They ___ eat vegetables. (a veces)

days.grammar: In, on, at, between, above, below, in front of, behind

examples.en: The book is on the table.
examples.es: El libro está en la mesa.

examples.en: She is in the kitchen.
examples.es: Ella está en la cocina.

examples.en: The dog is under the chair.
examples.es: El perro está bajo la silla.

days.tip: English has very specific prepositions. 'In' the kitchen, 'on' the table, 'at' the corner. Spanish is more flexible!

quiz.q: The book is ___ the table.
She is ___ the kitchen.
The dog is ___ the chair.

days.grammar: Narrative using past simple and past continuous

examples.en: I was walking in the park when I saw my friend. She was reading a book.
examples.es: Caminaba en el parque cuando vi a mi amiga. Estaba leyendo un libro.

examples.en: They were playing football when it started to rain. So they went home.
examples.es: Estaban jugando fútbol cuando comenzó a llover. Así que fueron a casa.

examples.en: While she was cooking, the phone rang. First she called back, then she finished cooking.
examples.es: Mientras cocinaba, sonó el teléfono. Primero devolvió la llamada, luego terminó de cocinar.

days.tip: Past continuous sets the scene (was doing), past simple interrupts (did). Use 'when' and 'while' to connect!

flashcards.front: Estaba caminando cuando vi a un amigo.
flashcards.back: I was walking when I saw a friend.

flashcards.front: Mientras ella cocinaba, sonó el teléfono.
flashcards.back: While she was cooking, the phone rang.

quiz.q: I ___ walking when I ___ my friend.
While she ___ cooking, the phone ___. (cocinaba/sonó)
They were playing ___ it started to rain.

days.grammar: Prepositions of time, direction, manner

examples.en: I go to school by bus.
examples.es: Voy a la escuela en autobús.

examples.en: She works with her family.
examples.es: Ella trabaja con su familia.

examples.en: We talk about sports every day.
examples.es: Hablamos de deportes todos los días.

days.tip: 'Talk about', 'work with', 'go by bus'. These are fixed phrases! Learn them as chunks, not individual words.

quiz.q: I talk ___ sports.
She works ___ her family.
We go to school ___ bus.

days.grammar: Review: adverbs of frequency, prepositions, past narrative

examples.en: I usually go to work by bus. The book is on the table. While I was working, my friend called.
examples.es: Usualmente voy al trabajo en autobús. El libro está en la mesa. Mientras trabajaba, mi amigo llamó.

examples.en: She always drinks coffee in the morning. She is at the corner. When she saw me, she was reading.
examples.es: Ella siempre bebe café por la mañana. Ella está en la esquina. Cuando me vio, estaba leyendo.

examples.en: They never eat vegetables at home. The dog is under the table. Because it rained, they stayed inside.
examples.es: Nunca comen verduras en casa. El perro está bajo la mesa. Porque llovió, se quedaron adentro.

days.tip: Frequency adverbs go before main verb or after 'to be'. Prepositions are fixed phrases. Remember them!

flashcards.back: I am always here (to be + adv)

flashcards.front: El libro está en la mesa. ¿Preposición?

quiz.q: I ___ drink coffee. (siempre)
The book is ___ the table.
While I ___ cooking, she ___. (cocinaba/llegó)

days.grammar: must (obligation, strong) vs have to (obligation, external)

examples.en: You must study for the exam.
examples.es: Debes estudiar para el examen.

examples.en: I have to go to work on Monday.
examples.es: Tengo que ir al trabajo el lunes.

examples.en: You must not use your phone in class.
examples.es: No debes usar tu teléfono en clase.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: 'must' is stronger than 'have to'. Use 'must' for rules and strong obligations, 'have to' for external circumstances.

flashcards.back: Los estudiantes deben llegar a tiempo

quiz.q: I _____ go to the doctor tomorrow because I'm sick.
You _____ smoke in the hospital.
Children _____ wear a seatbelt in cars (rule).

days.grammar: should (advice), could (possibility), might (possibility)

examples.en: You should drink more water.
examples.es: Deberías beber más agua.

examples.en: She might come to the party tomorrow.
examples.es: Ella podría venir a la fiesta mañana.

examples.en: You could try taking a different route.
examples.es: Podrías intentar un camino diferente.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: 'should' gives advice, 'could' and 'might' show possibility. Don't confuse with 'would' (conditional).

flashcards.front: You could apply for that job

quiz.q: I think you _____ apologize to her.
It _____ be sunny tomorrow, but I'm not sure.
You _____ exercise every day to stay healthy.

days.grammar: should, could, why don't you, how about, let me suggest

examples.en: Why don't you take a break?
examples.es: ¿Por qué no tomas un descanso?

examples.en: I'd suggest calling them first.
examples.es: Te sugiero que los llames primero.

examples.en: How about trying the new restaurant?
examples.es: ¿Qué tal si intentas el nuevo restaurante?

days.tip: Spanish speakers: Use 'why don't you' and 'how about' for softer suggestions. These are more polite in English than direct commands.

quiz.q: _____ you join us for dinner tonight?
I _____ reading that book. It's very good.
_____ we go to the cinema instead?

days.grammar: can, can't, may, may not, be allowed to, be permitted to

examples.en: You can use the library until 8 PM.
examples.es: Puedes usar la biblioteca hasta las 20 h.

examples.en: Smoking is not allowed in the office.
examples.es: Fumar no está permitido en la oficina.

examples.en: May I ask you a question?
examples.es: ¿Puedo hacerte una pregunta?

days.tip: Spanish speakers: 'May' is more formal than 'can' for asking permission. Use 'can' with friends, 'may' with strangers or authority figures.

quiz.q: _____ I use your bathroom?
Dogs are _____ allowed in this restaurant.
You _____ park here without a permit.

days.grammar: Review: must, have to, should, could, might, can, may

examples.en: You must arrive on time, and you should bring your ID.
examples.es: Debes llegar a tiempo, y deberías traer tu identificación.

examples.en: Can I come? Yes, you can. You could also bring a friend.
examples.es: ¿Puedo venir? Sí, puedes. También podrías traer a un amigo.

examples.en: He might not finish today, but he should try his best.
examples.es: Podría no terminar hoy, pero debería hacer su mejor esfuerzo.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: Practice these distinctions - they don't always match Spanish modal verbs perfectly. Context matters!

quiz.q: Which is the strongest obligation?
She _____ come to work tomorrow because she's sick.
You _____ study if you want to pass the exam.

days.grammar: have/has + past participle, present relevance

examples.en: I have visited Paris three times.
examples.es: He visitado París tres veces.

examples.en: She has finished her homework.
examples.es: Ella ha terminado su tarea.

examples.en: We have known each other for 10 years.
examples.es: Nos conocemos desde hace 10 años.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: Present perfect in English uses 'have/has' but Spanish uses 'he/has/han'. Don't forget the 'have'!

quiz.q: I _____ my keys. I can't find them!
She _____ to London twice.
They _____ for two hours.

days.grammar: ever (question/negative), never (negative), just (recent), already (completed)

examples.en: Have you ever been to Japan?
examples.es: ¿Alguna vez has estado en Japón?

examples.en: I have never tried sushi.
examples.es: Nunca he probado sushi.

examples.en: I have just finished my work.
examples.es: Acabo de terminar mi trabajo.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: 'Ever' = alguna vez (any time in life). 'Just' = acabo de (very recent). 'Already' = ya (completed).

quiz.q: Have you _____ eaten Mexican food?
I have _____ finished this book!
He has _____ been to Italy. It's still on his list.

days.grammar: for (duration), since (starting point), with present perfect

examples.en: I have worked here for 5 years.
examples.es: He trabajado aquí durante 5 años.

examples.en: She has lived in London since 2015.
examples.es: Ha vivido en Londres desde 2015.

examples.en: How long have you known him? For 10 years.
examples.es: ¿Cuánto tiempo lo conoces? 10 años.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: 'For' = período de tiempo. 'Since' = punto de partida en el tiempo. Both need present perfect in English.

flashcards.front: She has been here since 8 AM
flashcards.back: Ella ha estado aquí desde las 8 de la mañana

flashcards.front: How long have you lived here?
We have known each other since school

quiz.q: I have been a teacher _____ 15 years.
She has lived in Paris _____ 2010.
How long have you been married? _____ 20 years.

days.grammar: Present perfect (recent/unfinished), past simple (specific time/finished)

examples.en: I have lost my phone. (recent) vs. I lost my phone yesterday. (specific time)
examples.es: He perdido mi teléfono vs. Perdí mi teléfono ayer.

examples.en: Have you eaten? (general) vs. Did you eat breakfast? (specific)
examples.es: ¿Has comido? vs. ¿Comiste desayuno?

examples.en: She has worked here (still working) vs. She worked there last year (not anymore).
examples.es: Ha trabajado aquí vs. Trabajó allí el año pasado.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: Use present perfect for 'recently' or 'still happening'. Use past simple for 'specific past time'.

flashcards.back: Comí a las 6 de la tarde

quiz.q: I _____ three coffees this morning.
They _____ the meeting two hours ago.
_____ you ever visited Spain?

days.grammar: Review all present perfect uses and timing words

examples.en: I have never met anyone as kind as her.
examples.es: Nunca he conocido a alguien tan amable como ella.

examples.en: Have they arrived yet? They just left an hour ago.
examples.es: ¿Ya llegaron? Acaban de irse hace una hora.

examples.en: She has been working since 8 AM and has already finished two projects.
examples.es: Ha estado trabajando desde las 8 y ya ha terminado dos proyectos.

days.tip: Spanish speakers: Remember the 'have/has' is essential in English. Don't drop it like you might in Spanish!

quiz.q: Which is correct? (A) I have been living here since three years (B) I have lived here for three years
She has worked _____ 2020 without a break.
Have you ever _____ this kind of food before?

days.grammar: First conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb

examples.en: If it rains, I will stay home.
examples.es: Si llueve, me quedaré en casa.

examples.en: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
examples.es: Si estudias duro, pasarás el examen.

examples.en: I won't go unless you come with me.
examples.es: No iré a menos que vengas conmigo.

days.tip: First conditional = real/possible future. 'If + present, will + base.' Never say 'If I will...'!

quiz.q: 'If it ___, I will take an umbrella.'
'If you don't hurry, you ___ late.'
Choose the correct first conditional:
options: If I will go, I see her
If I go, I will see her
If I went, I will see
If I go, I would see

days.grammar: Present continuous for future plans

examples.en: I'm meeting my friend tomorrow at 3.
examples.es: Me reuniré con mi amigo mañana a las 3.

examples.en: Are you doing anything this weekend?
examples.es: ¿Vas a hacer algo este fin de semana?

examples.en: She's flying to Madrid next Monday.
examples.es: Ella vuela a Madrid el próximo lunes.

days.tip: English uses present continuous for confirmed plans: 'I'm having dinner with Ana tonight' (already arranged).

flashcards.back: What are you doing this weekend?

quiz.q: 'I ___ dinner with friends tonight.' (arranged plan)
'___ you ___ anything tomorrow?'
Which expresses a fixed plan?

days.grammar: Will for spontaneous decisions, offers, promises

examples.en: I'll help you carry that. (offer)
examples.es: Te ayudo a cargar eso.

examples.en: I promise I'll call you tomorrow.
examples.es: Te prometo que te llamaré mañana.

examples.en: Oh no, I forgot my wallet. I'll pay you back tomorrow.
examples.es: Ay no, olvidé mi billetera. Te pago mañana.

days.tip: Will for instant decisions: 'I'll have the chicken' (deciding now). Going to for pre-made plans.

quiz.q: Someone drops their books. You say:
'I ___ you I'll be there on time.'
'will' for spontaneous decisions vs 'going to' for:

days.grammar: In, on, at, by, until, during for future events

examples.en: I'll finish by Friday.
examples.es: Terminaré para el viernes.

examples.en: I'll wait until you arrive.
examples.es: Esperaré hasta que llegues.

examples.en: As soon as I get home, I'll call you.
examples.es: Tan pronto como llegue a casa, te llamo.

days.tip: 'By' = antes de un límite. 'Until' = desde ahora hasta ese momento. 'By Friday' vs 'Until Friday' are different!

quiz.q: 'I need this report ___ Monday.' (deadline)
'I'll stay here ___ you come back.'
'___ I arrive, I'll text you.'

days.grammar: Review: first conditional, plans, will vs going to, time expressions

examples.en: If the weather is nice, I'm going to the beach. I'm meeting Ana there at 2.
examples.es: Si el clima está bueno, voy a la playa. Me encuentro con Ana allí a las 2.

examples.en: I'll finish the project by Friday if nothing goes wrong.
examples.es: Terminaré el proyecto para el viernes si nada sale mal.

examples.en: I promise I'll call you as soon as I arrive.
examples.es: Te prometo que te llamo tan pronto como llegue.

days.tip: Remember: If + present (NOT will), going to for plans, will for instant decisions/promises/offers.

flashcards.back: As soon as I arrive, I'll call

quiz.q: 'If she ___, tell her I called.'
'I don't know what to eat... I ___ the pasta.'
'We ___ dinner at Luigi's tonight.' (booked)

days.grammar: Was/were + verb-ing

examples.en: I was watching TV when the phone rang.
examples.es: Estaba viendo televisión cuando sonó el teléfono.

examples.en: They were playing soccer at 5pm yesterday.
examples.es: Estaban jugando fútbol a las 5pm ayer.

examples.en: She was cooking while he was cleaning.
examples.es: Ella estaba cocinando mientras él estaba limpiando.

days.tip: Past continuous = estaba + gerundio. It's very similar to Spanish! 'Estaba comiendo' = 'I was eating.'

quiz.q: 'She ___ TV at 9pm last night.'
'They ___ soccer when it started raining.'
Past continuous uses:

days.grammar: When + past simple, While + past continuous

examples.en: While I was walking, I saw a cat.
examples.es: Mientras caminaba, vi un gato.

examples.en: When the teacher arrived, students were talking.
examples.es: Cuando llegó la profesora, los estudiantes estaban hablando.

examples.en: I was sleeping when the alarm went off.
examples.es: Estaba dormido cuando sonó la alarma.

days.tip: 'When' + short action (interruption), 'While' + long action (background). Same as cuando/mientras in Spanish!

quiz.q: '___ I was cooking, the phone rang.'
'I met her ___ I was living in London.'
'___ she called, I was sleeping.'

days.grammar: Combining past simple and past continuous in narratives

examples.en: First, I was walking home. Suddenly, it started raining. Then I ran to a café.
examples.es: Primero, estaba caminando a casa. De repente, empezó a llover. Luego corrí a un café.

examples.en: Meanwhile, my friend was waiting for me at the restaurant.
examples.es: Mientras tanto, mi amigo estaba esperándome en el restaurante.

examples.en: Eventually, the rain stopped and I finally arrived.
examples.es: Finalmente, la lluvia paró y por fin llegué.

days.tip: Use sequence words to make stories flow: first, then, after that, suddenly, meanwhile, finally.

quiz.q: Choose the correct combination:
options: I walked when it was raining
I was walking when it started raining
I was walking when it was starting to rain
I walked when it started to raining

quiz.q: '___, the bus arrived and we got on.'
'___, my sister was at home studying.'

days.grammar: Past continuous for background descriptions

examples.en: It was a beautiful morning. Birds were singing and the sun was shining.
examples.es: Era una mañana hermosa. Los pájaros cantaban y el sol brillaba.

examples.en: The restaurant was busy. People were talking and laughing.
examples.es: El restaurante estaba lleno. La gente hablaba y se reía.

examples.en: Everyone was having a great time at the party.
examples.es: Todos la estaban pasando bien en la fiesta.

days.tip: Use past continuous to 'paint a picture' of a scene — what was happening around, the atmosphere.

quiz.q: 'The sun ___ and birds ___.' (scene)
Past continuous for scenes is like:
'People ___ and ___ at the festival.'

days.grammar: Review: past continuous, when/while, storytelling, scene descriptions

examples.en: While I was walking to work, I saw an accident. The cars were blocking the road. I called the police and waited.
examples.es: Mientras caminaba al trabajo, vi un accidente. Los carros bloqueaban la calle. Llamé a la policía y esperé.

examples.en: It was a cold night. Snow was falling. Suddenly, I heard a noise.
examples.es: Era una noche fría. La nieve caía. De repente, escuché un ruido.

examples.en: First I was cooking. Then the phone rang. After that, I forgot about the food!
examples.es: Primero estaba cocinando. Luego sonó el teléfono. ¡Después me olvidé de la comida!

days.tip: Practice writing a short story about your last weekend using past simple + past continuous!

quiz.q: 'I ___ when he ___.'
'___ we were eating, the lights went out.'
To set a scene, use:

days.grammar: Countable vs uncountable quantity words

examples.en: There isn't much time left.
examples.es: No queda mucho tiempo.

examples.en: How many students are in your class?
examples.es: ¿Cuántos estudiantes hay en tu clase?

examples.en: She has a lot of friends.
examples.es: Ella tiene muchos amigos.

days.tip: 'Much' = uncountable (much water), 'Many' = countable (many books). 'A lot of' works for both!

quiz.q: 'How ___ water do you drink per day?'
'There are ___ people at the concert.'
'much' is used with:

days.topic: Few / Little vs A Few / A Little
days.grammar: Positive and negative meaning of quantity

examples.en: I have a few friends here. (positive — some)
examples.es: Tengo algunos amigos aquí.

examples.en: I have few friends here. (negative — not many)
examples.es: Tengo pocos amigos aquí.

examples.en: There's a little milk in the fridge.
examples.es: Hay un poco de leche en el refrigerador.

days.tip: 'A few/a little' = positive (enough). 'Few/little' = negative (not enough). Big difference!

quiz.q: 'I have ___ money, so I can buy lunch.' (positive)
'___ students passed the test.' (negative, not many)
'a few' vs 'few': 'a few' is:

days.grammar: Too + adjective, adjective + enough, enough + noun

examples.en: This coffee is too hot to drink.
examples.es: Este café está demasiado caliente para beber.

examples.en: She's old enough to drive.
examples.es: Ella tiene edad suficiente para conducir.

examples.en: There are too many people in this room.
examples.es: Hay demasiada gente en este cuarto.

days.tip: 'Too' goes BEFORE adjective: 'too hot.' 'Enough' goes AFTER adjective: 'old enough.' Opposite of Spanish order!

quiz.q: 'The water is ___ cold to swim.'
'She isn't tall ___ to reach the shelf.'
'too' goes ___ the adjective:

days.grammar: Something, anything, nothing, someone, etc.

examples.en: Is there anything I can help with?
examples.es: ¿Hay algo en lo que pueda ayudar?

examples.en: Nobody knows the answer.
examples.es: Nadie sabe la respuesta.

examples.en: I want to go somewhere warm.
examples.es: Quiero ir a algún lugar cálido.

days.tip: 'Some' in positive, 'any' in negative/questions. But 'some' in offers: 'Would you like something to drink?'

quiz.q: 'Is there ___ in the box?'
'___ called while you were out.'
'I have ___ to do today.' (nothing at all)

days.grammar: Review: quantity words, too/enough, some/any compounds

examples.en: There isn't much time and there are too many tasks. I need a few more hours.
examples.es: No hay mucho tiempo y hay demasiadas tareas. Necesito unas horas más.

examples.en: Is there anything to eat? There's a little rice and some chicken.
examples.es: ¿Hay algo de comer? Hay un poco de arroz y algo de pollo.

examples.en: The room is big enough but it's too noisy.
examples.es: El cuarto es suficientemente grande pero es demasiado ruidoso.

days.tip: You're almost done with A2! Practice using these quantity words in your daily conversations.

quiz.q: 'There isn't ___ milk left.'
'She's not ___ enough to enter.' (age)
'Would you like ___ to drink?'

days.grammar: Essential phrasal verbs for everyday English

examples.en: I get up at 7 every morning.
examples.es: Me levanto a las 7 cada mañana.

examples.en: Don't give up! Keep trying.
examples.es: ¡No te rindas! Sigue intentando.

examples.en: Can you look after my cat this weekend?
examples.es: ¿Puedes cuidar a mi gato este fin de semana?

days.tip: Phrasal verbs are verb + preposition combos that change meaning. They don't exist in Spanish, so learn them as units!

quiz.q: 'I need to ___ my keys. I lost them.'
'Please ___ the lights when you leave.'
'Don't ___! You can do it!'

days.grammar: Practical dialogue practice

examples.en: I've had a headache for two days.
examples.es: He tenido dolor de cabeza por dos días.

examples.en: Are you allergic to any medicine?
examples.es: ¿Eres alérgico a algún medicamento?

examples.en: I'll write you a prescription.
examples.es: Le escribiré una receta.

days.tip: At the doctor, use present perfect for symptoms: 'I've had a cough for a week' (started in past, still happening).

quiz.q: 'I've ___ a fever since yesterday.'
What is 'dolor de estómago'?
'Are you ___ to penicillin?'

days.grammar: Telephone English and leaving messages

examples.en: Hello, this is Maria speaking.
examples.es: Hola, habla María.

examples.en: Could you hold on a moment, please?
examples.es: ¿Podría esperar un momento, por favor?

examples.en: Can I leave a message?
examples.es: ¿Puedo dejar un mensaje?

days.tip: English phone etiquette: 'This is [name] speaking' not 'I am [name].' 'Hold on' = wait a moment.

quiz.q: 'Hello, ___ is John speaking.'
'Could you ___ for a moment?'
'I'll ___ you ___ later.'

days.grammar: More Spanish-English false friends and common errors

examples.en: 'Realize' means 'darse cuenta', NOT 'realizar' (= achieve/carry out).
examples.es: 'Realize' = darse cuenta. 'Carry out' = realizar.

examples.en: 'Attend' means 'asistir a', NOT 'atender' (= serve/take care of).
examples.es: 'Attend' = asistir a. 'Serve' = atender.

examples.en: 'Sympathetic' means 'comprensivo', NOT 'simpático' (= nice/friendly).
examples.es: 'Sympathetic' = comprensivo. 'Nice' = simpático.

days.tip: More false friends! These are tricky because they look SO similar to Spanish words.

flashcards.back: realize = darse cuenta; carry out = realizar

quiz.q: 'Realize' in Spanish means:
'Attend' in Spanish means:
'Sympathetic' does NOT mean:

days.grammar: Complete A2 review and celebration

examples.en: Congratulations! You completed A2! You can now use conditionals, present perfect, past continuous, and have real conversations.
examples.es: ¡Felicidades! ¡Completaste A2! Ahora puedes usar condicionales, presente perfecto, pasado continuo, y tener conversaciones reales.

examples.en: Next stop: B1 Intermediate! You'll learn more complex grammar.
examples.es: ¡Próxima parada: B1 Intermedio! Aprenderás gramática más compleja.

examples.en: Keep practicing every day!
examples.es: ¡Sigue practicando todos los días!

days.tip: 🎓 A2 COMPLETE! You've added: modals, present perfect, first conditional, past continuous, phrasal verbs. Ready for B1!

flashcards.back: Use modals, conditionals, tell stories, have real conversations
Present continuous, modals, present perfect, first conditional, past continuous
Past perfect, second conditional, passive, reported speech
get up, look for, turn on, give up, look after

quiz.q: You completed level:
In A2 you learned:
What's the next level?

levels.name: B1 - Intermediate
levels.description: Intermediate level English for Spanish speakers

days.grammar: PP (recent) vs PS (specific time/finished)

examples.en: I have written three emails today. I wrote them this morning.
examples.es: He escrito tres correos hoy. Los escribí esta mañana.

examples.en: She has lived here for 5 years. She moved in 2019.
examples.es: Ha vivido aquí durante 5 años. Se mudó en 2019.

examples.en: Have you finished? Did you finish yesterday?
examples.es: ¿Has terminado? ¿Terminaste ayer?

days.tip: B1: PP shows current relevance, PS shows past event with no connection to now.

quiz.q: I _____ my keys. I _____ them yesterday.
She _____ to five countries. She _____ to Spain last year.
They _____ at 3 PM. They _____ all afternoon.

days.grammar: just (very recent), already (by now), yet (expecting soon)

examples.en: I have just finished. She has already left. Have you arrived yet?
examples.es: Acabo de terminar. Ella ya se ha ido. ¿Ya has llegado?

examples.en: They have just arrived but they've already eaten.
examples.es: Acaban de llegar pero ya han comido.

examples.en: Is the project done yet? Not yet, but we're almost finished.
examples.es: ¿Está el proyecto terminado? Todavía no, pero casi hemos terminado.

days.tip: B1: 'Just' goes with recent PP. 'Already' shows sooner than expected. 'Yet' in questions/negatives.

quiz.q: I have _____ arrived. You've _____ been waiting for 10 minutes.
Have they left _____? They _____ gone 5 minutes ago.
The meeting has _____ started. Are you here _____?

days.grammar: have been + -ing (duration from past to now)

examples.en: I have been studying English for 3 months.
examples.es: He estado estudiando inglés durante 3 meses.

examples.en: How long have you been working here? Since January.
examples.es: ¿Cuánto tiempo has estado trabajando aquí? Desde enero.

examples.en: She's been waiting for an hour. Is she still waiting?
examples.es: Ha estado esperando durante una hora. ¿Todavía espera?

days.tip: B1: PP continuous emphasizes the duration and ongoing nature of actions.

flashcards.front: How long have you been here?
I've been here for a week

quiz.q: I _____ for this job for two weeks.
_____ you _____ this exercise? For 30 minutes.
They _____ on the project _____ three months.

days.grammar: All variations and their use cases

examples.en: I have worked here. I have been working here for 5 years. I worked there before.
examples.es: He trabajado aquí. He estado trabajando aquí durante 5 años. Trabajé allí antes.

examples.en: She has finished her task. She has been finishing it all morning. She finished it yesterday.
examples.es: Ha terminado su tarea. Ha estado terminándola toda la mañana. La terminó ayer.

examples.en: How long have you lived here? I've lived here for 10 years. I moved here in 2014.
examples.es: ¿Cuánto tiempo has vivido aquí? He vivido aquí durante 10 años. Me mudé aquí en 2014.

days.tip: B1: Listen for time references - 'for/since' = PP, 'yesterday/last week' = PS, 'still' = continuous.

quiz.q: I _____ at this company for five years, but I _____ elsewhere before.
How many times _____ you _____ this movie? I _____ it three times last month.
She _____ to Paris twice. She _____ there last year and _____ there again this spring.

days.grammar: Present perfect, PP continuous, distinction from past simple

examples.en: I have studied three languages. I have been studying Spanish for 5 years. I studied French in school.
examples.es: He estudiado tres idiomas. He estado estudiando español durante 5 años. Estudié francés en la escuela.

examples.en: Have you been waiting long? No, just arrived. When did you get here? 10 minutes ago.
examples.es: ¿Llevas mucho tiempo esperando? No, acabo de llegar. ¿Cuándo llegaste? Hace 10 minutos.

examples.en: She has completed the project. She has been working on it since Monday. She finished the report yesterday.
examples.es: Ha completado el proyecto. Ha estado trabajando en él desde el lunes. Completó el informe ayer.

days.tip: B1: Perfect tenses connect past to present. Master these for fluent speech.

quiz.q: Which sentence is wrong? (A) I have been living here for 5 years (B) I lived here for 5 years and I still live here
She _____ her homework when her mother _____ home.
They _____ in London _____ 2010.

weeks.title: Used To & Would for Past Habits

days.grammar: used to + base verb (repeated past action, no longer true)

examples.en: I used to play tennis every weekend. Now I don't.
examples.es: Solía jugar tenis cada fin de semana. Ahora no.

examples.en: She used to live in Barcelona. She moved to Madrid five years ago.
examples.es: Solía vivir en Barcelona. Se mudó a Madrid hace cinco años.

examples.en: We used to go camping every summer when we were kids.
examples.es: Solíamos ir de camping cada verano cuando éramos niños.

days.tip: B1: 'Used to' shows past habit that doesn't happen anymore. 'Now' implies change.

quiz.q: I _____ eat fast food every day, but now I eat healthier.
_____ you _____ play football in school?
She _____ visit her grandmother on weekends. Now she lives too far away.

days.grammar: would + base verb (habitual past, often with 'when')

examples.en: When I was young, I would go to the beach every summer.
examples.es: Cuando era joven, iría a la playa cada verano.

examples.en: Whenever it rained, the children would play inside.
examples.es: Cada vez que llovía, los niños jugarían adentro.

examples.en: We would always meet at the same cafe. It was our routine.
examples.es: Siempre nos reuniríamos en el mismo café. Era nuestra rutina.

days.tip: B1: 'Would' is like 'used to' but emphasizes the repeated routine. 'Would' can't be used for states.

quiz.q: When I lived in Paris, I _____ to cafes every evening.
Whenever she visited, we _____ the same restaurant.
As a child, he _____ spend hours reading in the library.

days.grammar: Combining used to and would for narrative

examples.en: I remember when we used to live by the sea. We would walk on the beach every morning.
examples.es: Recuerdo cuando solíamos vivir junto al mar. Caminábamos por la playa cada mañana.

examples.en: Back then, I used to have more free time. I would spend it with friends every weekend.
examples.es: En esos días, solía tener más tiempo libre. Lo pasaba con amigos cada fin de semana.

examples.en: Do you remember how we used to play in the park? We would stay there until sunset.
examples.es: ¿Recuerdas cómo solíamos jugar en el parque? Nos quedábamos allí hasta la puesta de sol.

days.tip: B1: Mix 'used to' and 'would' for richer storytelling about the past.

flashcards.back: Use both used to and would

quiz.q: I _____ live near the mountains. I _____ hike every weekend.
When I was a student, I _____ stay up late and _____ study with friends.
Remember when you _____ have longer hair? You _____ always tie it up.

days.topic: Contrast: Used To vs Would vs Past Simple
days.grammar: Differences and when to use each

examples.en: I used to be shy. (state) I would practice speaking. (routine) I spoke at the conference once. (single event)
examples.es: Solía ser tímido. (estado) Practicaba hablar. (rutina) Hablé en la conferencia una vez. (evento único)

examples.en: She used to live in Spain. (past state) She would visit every summer. (routine) She moved last year. (single event)
examples.es: Solía vivir en España. (estado pasado) Visitaba cada verano. (rutina) Se mudó el año pasado. (evento único)

examples.en: They used to have a car. They would drive everywhere. They drove to the coast one weekend.
examples.es: Solían tener un coche. Conducían a todas partes. Condujeron a la costa un fin de semana.

days.tip: B1: Used to can be states or habits. Would only habits. PS for specific, completed events.

quiz.q: Which is NOT correct? (A) I used to smoke (B) I would smoke every day (C) I smoke yesterday
He _____ work as a teacher. He _____ teach 30 students. He _____ quit in 2015.
She _____ have long hair when she _____ younger. She _____ cut it short last month.

days.grammar: Mastery of used to, would, and past simple in context

examples.en: I used to work in a bank. I would arrive at 8 AM every day. One day I quit and started my own business.
examples.es: Trabajaba en un banco. Llegaba a las 8 AM cada día. Un día renuncié e inicié mi propio negocio.

examples.en: When I was a student, I used to study hard. I would spend hours in the library. Then I graduated and started working.
examples.es: Cuando era estudiante, estudiaba mucho. Pasaba horas en la biblioteca. Luego me gradué y comencé a trabajar.

examples.en: We used to be best friends. We would meet every week. Now we hardly talk to each other.
examples.es: Solíamos ser mejores amigos. Nos reuníamos cada semana. Ahora casi no nos hablamos.

days.tip: B1: These tenses make you sound more natural when talking about your past.

flashcards.back: Past condition (I used to be thin)

quiz.q: Which is grammatically correct? (A) I would be tired (B) I used to be tired (C) Both
I _____ shy when I _____ young. I _____ have difficulty speaking. That _____ change over time.
Every summer, we _____ to the lake. We _____ camp there for two weeks. This year, _____ go somewhere else.

days.grammar: if + past, would + infinitive (unreal, present)

examples.en: If I were rich, I would travel the world.
examples.es: Si fuera rico, viajaría por el mundo.

examples.en: If you studied more, you would pass the exam.
examples.es: Si estudiaras más, aprobarías el examen.

examples.en: What would you do if you won the lottery?
examples.es: ¿Qué harías si ganaras la lotería?

days.tip: B1: Second conditional is imaginary NOW. Past form doesn't mean past time.

flashcards.front: If I had time, I would

quiz.q: If I _____ you, I _____ this job.
What _____ you do if you _____ a car?
If she _____ more time, she _____ finish the project.

days.grammar: wish + past (present regret), if only, I wish I could

examples.en: I wish I were taller.
examples.es: Ojalá fuera más alto.

examples.en: If only I had studied harder, I would have passed.
examples.es: Si solo hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado.

examples.en: I wish I could speak French fluently.
examples.es: Ojalá pudiera hablar francés con fluidez.

days.tip: B1: 'Wish' uses past tense for present regret. 'If only' = emphasizes wish even more.

quiz.q: I wish I _____ more languages.
If only I _____ rich!
She wishes she _____ more time to read.

days.grammar: Combining conditions and wishes naturally

examples.en: If I had more time, I would learn guitar. I wish I could practice more.
examples.es: Si tuviera más tiempo, aprendería guitarra. Ojalá pudiera practicar más.

examples.en: Unless you apologize, I won't forgive you. I wish you would understand my feelings.
examples.es: A menos que pidas disculpas, no te perdonaré. Ojalá entendieras mis sentimientos.

examples.en: Suppose you had a million dollars. What would you do? I wish I knew.
examples.es: Supongamos que tienes un millón de dólares. ¿Qué harías? Ojalá lo supiera.

days.tip: B1: Conditionals and wishes express dreams and regrets. Use them in conversation.

quiz.q: If I _____ my job, I _____ travel more. But I wish I _____.
Suppose your friend _____ late. What _____? I wish I _____ patience.
I wish you _____ understand. If you _____, everything _____ better.

days.grammar: Second conditional scenarios, making arguments

examples.en: If I were the boss, I would give everyone a raise. If the company were bigger, we'd have better benefits.
examples.es: Si fuera el jefe, daría a todos un aumento. Si la empresa fuera más grande, tendríamos mejores beneficios.

examples.en: Suppose you were in my position. What would you do? I'd probably make the same decision.
examples.es: Supongamos que estuvieras en mi posición. ¿Qué harías? Probablemente haría la misma decisión.

examples.en: If politicians actually listened to citizens, policy would change. If people voted more, things would be different.
examples.es: Si los políticos realmente escucharan a los ciudadanos, la política cambiaría. Si las personas votaran más, las cosas serían diferentes.

days.tip: B1: These situations let you express opinions and make arguments about hypothetical scenarios.

quiz.q: If you _____ the leader, what _____ you _____?
If the weather _____ better, we _____ go hiking.
Suppose you _____ a choice between two jobs. Which _____ you _____?

days.grammar: Complete B1 conditional mastery

examples.en: If I were taller, I would play basketball. I wish I could play professionally. If I practiced more, I might get better.
examples.es: Si fuera más alto, jugaría baloncesto. Ojalá pudiera jugar profesionalmente. Si practicara más, podría mejorar.

examples.en: What would you do if you lost your job? I wish I had better savings. If I did, I'd feel more secure.
examples.es: ¿Qué harías si perdieras tu trabajo? Ojalá tuviera mejores ahorros. Si lo tuviera, me sentiría más seguro.

examples.en: If I could change one thing about myself, it would be my confidence. I wish I weren't so shy. If I were braver, I'd take more risks.
examples.es: Si pudiera cambiar una cosa de mí, sería mi confianza. Ojalá no fuera tan tímido. Si fuera más valiente, tomaría más riesgos.

days.tip: B1: These express your personality, regrets, and dreams. Practice them daily.

quiz.q: Which is correct? (A) If I were you (B) If I was you (C) Both
I wish I _____ speak Chinese. If I could, I _____ study in Beijing.
If she _____ the truth, would she still be angry? I wish she _____ understand.

days.grammar: is/are + past participle, was/were + past participle

examples.en: The house was built in 2000. The book is written by a famous author.
examples.es: La casa fue construida en 2000. El libro es escrito por un autor famoso.

examples.en: Pasta is made from wheat.
examples.es: La pasta está hecha de trigo.

examples.en: The movie was directed by Spielberg.
examples.es: La película fue dirigida por Spielberg.

days.tip: B1: Passive is common in formal writing and news. Focus on past participle forms.

quiz.q: The Eiffel Tower _____ in Paris.
This book _____ by Stephen King.
The project _____ last week.

days.grammar: Present/past perfect, continuous passives

examples.en: The bridge has been built. It was being constructed last year.
examples.es: El puente ha sido construido. Se estaba construyendo el año pasado.

examples.en: The new stadium will be opened next month.
examples.es: El nuevo estadio será abierto el próximo mes.

examples.en: While the repairs were being made, the building was closed.
examples.es: Mientras se hacían las reparaciones, el edificio estaba cerrado.

days.tip: B1: Passive in different tenses follows same pattern. Memorize the helping verbs.

quiz.q: The report _____ yesterday and _____ today.
The house _____ _____ for months before we bought it.
The project _____ _____ _____ next week.

days.grammar: Recognizing active vs passive appropriately

examples.en: Active: Spielberg directed the movie. Passive: The movie was directed by Spielberg.
examples.es: Activo: Spielberg dirigió la película. Pasivo: La película fue dirigida por Spielberg.

examples.en: Active is better: John ate the pizza. (Passive sounds awkward: The pizza was eaten by John.)
examples.es: El activo es mejor: Juan comió la pizza. (El pasivo suena raro.)

examples.en: Passive is better in news: Three people were injured. Police are investigating.
examples.es: El pasivo es mejor en noticias: Tres personas resultaron heridas. La policía está investigando.

days.tip: B1: Use passive for news, formal writing, and when agent is unknown. Use active for clarity.

quiz.q: Which sentence is best? (A) The cake was eaten by me (B) I ate the cake
News sentence: A car _____ yesterday in downtown.
Formal: The experiment _____ in 1990.

days.grammar: can be done, should be done, must be completed

examples.en: The work should be done by Friday. The meeting must be postponed. The decision can be made later.
examples.es: El trabajo debería ser hecho para el viernes. La reunión debe ser pospuesta. La decisión puede ser tomada después.

examples.en: These documents might be needed. Your application should have been submitted earlier.
examples.es: Estos documentos podrían ser necesarios. Tu solicitud debería haber sido presentada antes.

examples.en: The mail should be opened carefully. The package must be handled with care.
examples.es: El correo debería ser abierto cuidadosamente. El paquete debe ser manipulado con cuidado.

days.tip: B1: Modal + be + past participle = passive with obligation/possibility.

quiz.q: This task _____ _____ by tomorrow.
The report _____ _____ carefully.
The meeting _____ _____ next week _____ necessary.

days.grammar: Complete passive mastery

examples.en: Active: Someone built this house. Passive: This house was built. Active: They make cars here. Passive: Cars are made here.
examples.es: Activo: Alguien construyó esta casa. Pasivo: Esta casa fue construida. Activo: Fabrican carros aquí. Pasivo: Los carros se fabrican aquí.

examples.en: The museum was opened in 1999. It has been visited by millions. It will be expanded next year.
examples.es: El museo fue abierto en 1999. Ha sido visitado por millones. Será expandido el próximo año.

examples.en: These books should be read by everyone. The author's work should be studied in schools.
examples.es: Estos libros deberían ser leídos por todos. La obra del autor debería ser estudiada en las escuelas.

days.tip: B1: Passive is essential for formal English and news reporting. Master the patterns.

quiz.q: Transform to passive: They discovered gold in 1848. Gold _____ in 1848.
Which is better in news? (A) Thieves stole the painting (B) The painting was stolen
The new hospital _____ _____ _____ 2025.

days.grammar: She said that..., He told me..., change of tense

examples.en: She said, 'I am tired.' → She said that she was tired.
examples.es: Ella dijo, 'Estoy cansada.' → Ella dijo que estaba cansada.

examples.en: He said, 'I will come tomorrow.' → He said that he would come the next day.
examples.es: Él dijo, 'Vendré mañana.' → Él dijo que vendría al día siguiente.

examples.en: They said, 'We have finished.' → They said that they had finished.
examples.es: Ellos dijeron, 'Hemos terminado.' → Ellos dijeron que habían terminado.

days.tip: B1: Tense shifts back one step. am/is → was, will → would, have → had.

quiz.q: Direct: 'I like pizza.' Reported: She _____ that she _____ pizza.
Direct: 'She is working.' Reported: He said that she _____ working.
Direct: 'I will call you.' Reported: She _____ she _____ call me.

days.grammar: ask if/whether, reported negative, did not, was not

examples.en: She asked, 'Do you like it?' → She asked if I liked it.
examples.es: Ella preguntó, '¿Te gusta?' → Ella preguntó si me gustaba.

examples.en: 'Are you coming?' he asked. → He asked if I was coming.
examples.es: '¿Vienes?' preguntó. → Él preguntó si estaba viniendo.

examples.en: 'I don't know the answer.' → She said that she didn't know the answer.
examples.es: 'No sé la respuesta.' → Ella dijo que no sabía la respuesta.

days.tip: B1: Questions use 'if/whether'. No question mark in reported form. Tenses still shift.

quiz.q: Direct: 'Can you help?' Reported: She _____ if I _____ help.
Direct: 'Have you finished?' Reported: He _____ if they _____ finished.
Direct: 'I won't be late.' Reported: She said she _____ be late.

days.grammar: now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day

examples.en: He said, 'I will see you tomorrow.' → He said he would see me the next day.
examples.es: Él dijo, 'Te veré mañana.' → Él dijo que me vería al día siguiente.

examples.en: 'Are you coming now?' she asked. → She asked if I was coming then.
examples.es: '¿Vienes ahora?' preguntó. → Ella preguntó si estaba viniendo entonces.

examples.en: 'I was there yesterday.' → He said he had been there the day before.
examples.es: 'Estaba allí ayer.' → Él dijo que había estado allí el día anterior.

days.tip: B1: Change time words based on when the report is made. this → that, here → there.

quiz.q: She said, 'I'll do it today.' → She said she _____ do it _____.
'I was there yesterday.' → He _____ he _____ been there _____ .
'See you tomorrow!' → He said he would see me _____ .

days.grammar: asked me to..., told me to..., instructions, imperatives

examples.en: 'Please help me.' → She asked me to help her.
examples.es: 'Por favor ayúdame.' → Ella me pidió que la ayudara.

examples.en: 'Don't be late!' → He told me not to be late.
examples.es: '¡No llegues tarde!' → Él me dijo que no llegara tarde.

examples.en: 'Listen carefully.' → The teacher instructed us to listen carefully.
examples.es: 'Escucha cuidadosamente.' → El maestro nos instruyó a escuchar cuidadosamente.

days.tip: B1: Imperatives → 'ask/tell + person + to + infinitive'. Negatives use 'not to'.

flashcards.back: Por favor espera → pidió que esperara

quiz.q: 'Can you help me?' → She _____ me _____ help her.
'Don't worry!' → He _____ me _____ worry.
'Leave immediately!' → He _____ them _____ immediately.

days.grammar: All reported speech patterns

examples.en: She said, 'I am working here. I'll stay for two years. Don't worry about me.' → She said that she was working there and that she would stay for two years, and she told me not to worry about her.
examples.es: Ella dijo, 'Estoy trabajando aquí. Me quedaré dos años. No te preocupes por mí.' → Ella dijo que estaba trabajando allí y que se quedaría dos años, y me dijo que no me preocupara.

examples.en: He asked, 'Are you coming tomorrow? When will you arrive?' → He asked if I was coming the next day and when I would arrive.
examples.es: Él preguntó, '¿Vienes mañana? ¿Cuándo llegarás?' → Él preguntó si venía al día siguiente y cuándo llegaría.

examples.en: 'Please call me. Don't forget!' she said. → She asked me to call her and told me not to forget.
examples.es: '¡Llámame! ¡No olvides!' dijo. → Ella me pidió que la llamara y me dijo que no olvidara.

days.tip: B1: Report what people said with confidence. Mix statements, questions, and requests.

quiz.q: Which is correct reported speech? (A) She said she is tired (B) She said she was tired
He said, 'I've finished.' → He _____ he _____ finished.
'Don't tell anyone!' → She _____ me _____ tell anyone.

days.topic: Who, Which, That for Defining Clauses
days.grammar: relative clauses with who (people), which (things), that (both)

examples.en: The woman who works here is my friend.
examples.es: La mujer que trabaja aquí es mi amiga.

examples.en: The book which I read was interesting.
examples.es: El libro que leí era interesante.

examples.en: The person that called me is my boss.
examples.es: La persona que me llamó es mi jefe.

days.tip: B1: 'Who' for people, 'which' for things, 'that' for both. These are essential to the meaning.

quiz.q: The student _____ won the award is very talented.
The book _____ she recommended is bestseller.
The man _____ I met yesterday is my new colleague.

days.topic: Where, When, Why in Relative Clauses
days.grammar: where (place), when (time), why (reason)

examples.en: The restaurant where we ate was expensive.
examples.es: El restaurante donde comimos era caro.

examples.en: The day when I met you was special.
examples.es: El día cuando te conocí fue especial.

examples.en: The reason why she left is unknown.
examples.es: La razón por la que se fue es desconocida.

days.tip: B1: 'Where'=lugar, 'when'=tiempo, 'why'=razón. Can also use 'in which', 'at which'.

quiz.q: The city _____ I was born is very beautiful.
Do you remember the time _____ we first met?
I don't understand the reason _____ you left.

days.grammar: whose (possession), whom (object), relative pronouns

examples.en: The woman whose car is outside is waiting.
examples.es: La mujer cuyo coche está afuera está esperando.

examples.en: The person whom you met is my boss.
examples.es: La persona a quien conociste es mi jefe.

examples.en: The students whose essays were best received prizes.
examples.es: Los estudiantes cuyos ensayos fueron los mejores recibieron premios.

days.tip: B1: 'Whose'=posesión, 'whom'=objeto (whom is becoming less common in spoken English).

quiz.q: The girl _____ parents are doctors wants to study medicine.
The actor _____ I saw in the movie is very famous.
The person _____ opinion matters most is the director.

days.grammar: Extra information with commas, not essential

examples.en: My sister, who lives in Spain, is a teacher. (The fact that she lives in Spain is extra info.)
examples.es: Mi hermana, que vive en España, es maestra. (Que vive en España es información extra.)

examples.en: The book, which I recommend, is fascinating.
examples.es: El libro, que recomiendo, es fascinante.

examples.en: London, where I spent five years, is an amazing city.
examples.es: Londres, donde pasé cinco años, es una ciudad increíble.

days.tip: B1: Non-defining clauses add info but aren't essential. Use commas. Can't use 'that'.

quiz.q: Which is correct? (A) My teacher who is kind (B) My teacher, who is kind,
The movie _____, which received awards, was brilliant.
Spain, _____ I lived for years, is my favorite country.

days.grammar: All relative clause types mastered

examples.en: The woman who called me, whose name is Sarah, works here. She's the one that everyone respects. The office where she works is new.
examples.es: La mujer que me llamó, cuyo nombre es Sarah, trabaja aquí. Es la que todos respetan. La oficina donde trabaja es nueva.

examples.en: My friend, who is very smart, passed the exam that was difficult. The grade, which was 95%, was impressive.
examples.es: Mi amigo, que es muy inteligente, aprobó el examen que era difícil. La calificación, que fue 95%, fue impresionante.

examples.en: The city where I grew up, whose history is rich, is now modern. The people who live there are friendly.
examples.es: La ciudad donde crecí, cuya historia es rica, ahora es moderna. La gente que vive allí es amable.
