days.tip: B1: Mix defining and non-defining. Vary your pronouns. Make your speech more sophisticated.

flashcards.front: Defining: who, which, that, where, when, why
Non-defining: who, which, where, when, whose

quiz.q: Combine: I know the girl. She won the prize. → The girl _____ _____ the prize is my friend.
Which uses 'that' correctly? (A) The book that I love (B) The book, that I love, (C) Both
My teacher, _____ is very helpful, has a class _____ many students attend.

days.grammar: gerunds as nouns, after certain verbs, after prepositions

examples.en: I enjoy playing tennis. She loves dancing. They avoid smoking.
examples.es: Disfruto jugando tenis. A ella le encanta bailar. Evitan fumar.

examples.en: Swimming is a great exercise. Reading helps you learn.
examples.es: Nadar es un gran ejercicio. Leer te ayuda a aprender.

examples.en: I'm interested in learning French. She's good at cooking.
examples.es: Estoy interesado en aprender francés. Ella es buena cocinando.

days.tip: B1: Gerunds are nouns. They can be the subject or object of a sentence.

quiz.q: I _____ _____ in the morning.
_____ English is useful.
She's interested in _____ art.

days.grammar: infinitive as noun, after certain verbs, with purpose

examples.en: I want to learn Spanish. She hopes to travel. They decided to move.
examples.es: Quiero aprender español. Ella espera viajar. Decidieron mudarse.

examples.en: To succeed, you need to work hard.
examples.es: Para tener éxito, necesitas trabajar duro.

examples.en: I'm trying to fix my computer. She managed to finish on time.
examples.es: Estoy intentando reparar mi computadora. Ella logró terminar a tiempo.

days.tip: B1: Infinitive expresses intentions, desires, and goals. After 'to'.

quiz.q: I _____ _____ to the party.
She _____ _____ her dream job.
They _____ _____ a new house.

days.grammar: Some verbs change meaning with gerund vs infinitive

examples.en: I remember meeting you. (A past event) vs. I remembered to call you. (I didn't forget to call.)
examples.es: Recuerdo haberte conocido. vs. Recordé llamarte. (No olvidé llamarte.)

examples.en: Stop drinking! (You're drinking now) vs. Stop to drink. (Take a break and drink)
examples.es: ¡Para de beber! vs. Para a beber. (Toma un descanso y bebe)

examples.en: I regret leaving. (I left and I'm sorry) vs. I regret to tell you. (I'm sorry to inform you)
examples.es: Lamento haberme ido. vs. Lamento informarte. (Siento decirte)

days.tip: B1: Meaning changes! Memorize these important pairs.

quiz.q: I _____ _____ you at the party. (A past event)
I _____ _____ to call you. (I didn't forget.)
She _____ _____ when she heard the news.

days.grammar: Complex sentences, perfect forms, passive forms

examples.en: Having finished my work, I left early.
examples.es: Habiendo terminado mi trabajo, me fui temprano.

examples.en: I regret not having studied harder.
examples.es: Lamento no haber estudiado más.

examples.en: Being told the news was shocking.
examples.es: Que me dijeran la noticia fue impactante.

days.tip: B1: Perfect gerunds and passive forms add sophistication to your speech.

quiz.q: _____ _____ the movie, I felt sad.
I appreciate _____ _____ the opportunity.
I regret not _____ _____ harder.

days.grammar: Master all forms and applications

examples.en: I enjoy reading, and I love to write. I tried studying, but I prefer to relax. I finished working, and I hope to rest.
examples.es: Disfruto leyendo, y me encanta escribir. Intenté estudiar, pero prefiero relajarme. Terminé trabajando, y espero descansar.

examples.en: Remember to call me. I remember calling you. Stop eating junk food. Stop to eat something healthy.
examples.es: Recuerda llamarme. Recuerdo haberte llamado. Para de comer comida chatarra. Para a comer algo saludable.

examples.en: Having completed the work, I went home. To have succeeded was my goal. Being praised felt wonderful.
examples.es: Habiendo completado el trabajo, me fui a casa. Haber tenido éxito fue mi objetivo. Ser elogiado se sintió maravilloso.

days.tip: B1: These patterns appear constantly. Mastering them will significantly improve your English.

quiz.q: I love _____ and I hate _____ .
Which changes meaning? (A) I like reading (B) I like to read (C) Both same
She _____ _____ the exam, so she's happy.

days.topic: Grammar Summary: Conditionals to Relative Clauses
days.grammar: Review weeks 1-7 key structures

examples.en: If I were richer, I would travel. The places I visit are amazing. People say that travel is life-changing. I enjoy traveling and love to explore.
examples.es: Si fuera más rico, viajaría. Los lugares que visito son increíbles. La gente dice que viajar es un cambio de vida. Disfruto viajar y me encanta explorar.

examples.en: I used to think London was boring, but the city that captivated me is actually fascinating. Structures that are reported by tourists include Big Ben.
examples.es: Solía pensar que Londres era aburrido, pero la ciudad que me cautivó es fascinante. Las estructuras que reportan los turistas incluyen Big Ben.

examples.en: She wishes she could go with us. The project, which has been finished, was reported to management. We're planning to celebrate.
examples.es: Ella desea poder venir con nosotros. El proyecto, que ha sido terminado, fue reportado a la gerencia. Estamos planeando celebrar.

days.tip: B1: Review all 7 weeks. These structures appear in real conversations.

flashcards.back: who, which, that, where, when, why

quiz.q: If I _____ the time, I _____ help you.
The building _____ _____ in 1990 is historic.
She said she _____ _____ her homework.

days.grammar: Integrated use of all B1 structures in dialogue

examples.en: A: Have you ever been to Peru? B: Yes, I've been there twice. A: What was it like? B: It was amazing! The ruins that I visited were incredible. A: Did you do anything special? B: I wish I had more time. I used to think it was just about Machu Picchu, but there's so much more.
examples.es: A: ¿Alguna vez has estado en Perú? B: Sí, he estado allí dos veces. A: ¿Cómo fue? B: ¡Fue increíble! Las ruinas que visité eran increíbles. A: ¿Hiciste algo especial? B: Ojalá hubiera tenido más tiempo. Solía pensar que era solo sobre Machu Picchu, pero hay mucho más.

examples.en: If I were you, I would go to the Amazon. It's an experience that will change your perspective. People who have been there say it's unforgettable. I'm hoping to return soon.
examples.es: Si fuera tú, iría a la Amazonía. Es una experiencia que cambiará tu perspectiva. La gente que ha estado allí dice que es inolvidable. Espero regresar pronto.

days.tip: B1: Use real experiences to practice. Tell stories combining multiple structures.

flashcards.back: I wish, if only, should have
Going to, hope to, planning to

quiz.q: A: Have you _____ to Rome? B: Yes, I _____ there twice.
The churches _____ we _____ were breathtaking.
I _____ I _____ stayed longer.

days.grammar: B1 structures in professional communication

examples.en: As requested, the project has been completed. The results, which exceed our expectations, are detailed below. It is reported that customer satisfaction has increased by 15%.
examples.es: Como se solicitó, el proyecto ha sido completado. Los resultados, que superan nuestras expectativas, se detallan a continuación. Se reporta que la satisfacción del cliente ha aumentado un 15%.

examples.en: The team, whose dedication is commendable, has finished the report that was assigned. In conclusion, the changes recommended should be implemented immediately.
examples.es: El equipo, cuya dedicación es encomiable, ha terminado el informe que fue asignado. En conclusión, los cambios recomendados deberían implementarse inmediatamente.

days.tip: B1: Formal writing uses passive voice and relative clauses extensively.

quiz.q: The report _____ _____ has been approved.
The findings, _____ important, are _____ in the document.
It is _____ that the project _____ _____ on time.

days.grammar: Combining all B1 structures naturally

examples.en: I was walking through the park when I found a wallet. The wallet, which contained ID and money, belonged to a man named Carlos. I called the number that was inside. He said he had been looking everywhere. I wish I could have seen his face when he realized!
examples.es: Estaba caminando por el parque cuando encontré una billetera. La billetera, que contenía identificación y dinero, pertenecía a un hombre llamado Carlos. Llamé al número que estaba adentro. Dijo que había estado buscando por todas partes. ¡Ojalá hubiera podido ver su cara cuando se dio cuenta!

examples.en: The story that changed my life began unexpectedly. If I hadn't been late that day, I would never have met her. As it happened, we became friends. She told me about her dreams, and I told her about mine. Both of us wish we had met earlier.
examples.es: La historia que cambió mi vida comenzó inesperadamente. Si no hubiera llegado tarde ese día, nunca la habría conocido. Como resultó, nos hicimos amigos. Ella me contó sobre sus sueños, y yo le conté los míos. Ambos deseamos habernos conocido antes.

days.tip: B1: Master these combinations for authentic, expressive English communication.

quiz.q: A: What _____ when you _____ ? B: I _____ walking when the alarm _____.
She told me she _____ _____ the news _____ _____ her life.
If he _____ me, I _____ sad. I wish he _____ understood.

days.grammar: Consolidation and forward look

examples.en: Congratulations! You have completed B1. You have mastered conditionals, passive voice, reported speech, and relative clauses. You can now discuss complex ideas and tell detailed stories using sophisticated grammar structures.
examples.es: ¡Felicidades! Has completado B1. Has dominado condicionales, voz pasiva, discurso reportado, y cláusulas relativas. Ahora puedes discutir ideas complejas y contar historias detalladas usando estructuras gramaticales sofisticadas.

examples.en: In B2, you will learn even more advanced topics: third conditionals, advanced passives, inversion, and advanced modals. These will help you express nuance and sophistication in English.
examples.es: En B2, aprenderás temas aún más avanzados: terceros condicionales, pasivos avanzados, inversión, y modales avanzados. Estos te ayudarán a expresar matices y sofisticación en inglés.

examples.en: Your B1 achievement shows you are ready. Keep practicing, keep reading, keep listening. English fluency is within reach!
examples.es: Tu logro B1 muestra que estás listo. ¡Sigue practicando, sigue leyendo, sigue escuchando! ¡La fluidez en inglés está al alcance!

days.tip: B1: You've built a strong intermediate foundation. You're ready for the next challenge.

flashcards.back: Perfect, passive, conditional, reported, relative, gerunds, infinitives
Discuss complex topics, tell stories, write formally

quiz.q: Which B1 topics were covered? (select all - conceptual)
What makes B2 different from B1?
Best way to continue learning?

levels.name: B2 - Upper Intermediate
levels.description: Upper intermediate level English for Spanish speakers

days.grammar: if + past perfect, would have + past participle

examples.en: If I had studied harder, I would have passed the exam.
examples.es: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado el examen.

examples.en: If she hadn't called, I wouldn't have known about it.
examples.es: Si ella no hubiera llamado, no lo habría sabido.

examples.en: If we had left earlier, we would have arrived on time.
examples.es: Si nos hubiéramos ido antes, habríamos llegado a tiempo.

days.tip: B2: Third conditional expresses impossible situations in the past. It's pure regret.

quiz.q: If he _____ the truth, he _____ angry.
If you _____ asked, I _____ told you.
She _____ _____ the mistake if she _____ more careful.

days.grammar: if + past perfect, would + present infinitive

examples.en: If I had gone to university, I would be a lawyer now.
examples.es: Si hubiera ido a la universidad, sería abogado ahora.

examples.en: If you hadn't met him, you wouldn't be married today.
examples.es: Si no lo hubieras conocido, no estarías casado hoy.

examples.en: If she had studied medicine, she would be a doctor.
examples.es: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, sería doctora.

days.tip: B2: Mixed conditionals show how past decisions still affect us now.

quiz.q: If I _____ abroad, I _____ speak English so well now.
She _____ a singer if she _____ harder as a child.
If you _____ that job, you _____ be rich now.

days.grammar: if + present/past, would have + past participle

examples.en: If you were braver, you would have taken that job.
examples.es: Si fueras más valiente, habrías aceptado ese trabajo.

examples.en: If he weren't so stubborn, he would have listened to our advice.
examples.es: Si no fuera tan terco, habría escuchado nuestro consejo.

examples.en: If I weren't so lazy, I would have finished the project.
examples.es: Si no fuera tan perezoso, habría terminado el proyecto.

days.tip: B2: These show character traits or conditions that affected past decisions.

quiz.q: If he _____ confident, he _____ asked her out.
She _____ passed if she _____ more intelligent.
If they _____ organized, they _____ finished earlier.

days.grammar: Chaining multiple conditions, layers of hypotheticals

examples.en: If you had worked harder and if I had helped you, we would have finished. On condition that he arrives, we'll start.
examples.es: Si hubieras trabajado más y si te hubiera ayudado, habríamos terminado. Siempre que llegue, comenzaremos.

examples.en: As long as the weather permits, and provided the team is ready, we'll proceed with the plan.
examples.es: Siempre que el clima lo permita, y siempre que el equipo esté listo, procederemos con el plan.

examples.en: Unless there are complications, and in case we need extra help, I'll contact you.
examples.es: A menos que haya complicaciones, y en caso de que necesitemos ayuda extra, te contactaré.

days.tip: B2: Chain conditionals for complex, realistic scenarios.

quiz.q: I'll help _____ you _____ effort. In that case, I _____ grateful.
On condition _____ you _____ honest, I'll trust you.
She'll come _____ the weather _____ bad, and _____ that case, we'll reschedule.

days.grammar: All three conditionals, mixed types, integration

examples.en: First: If you study, you will pass. Second: If you studied, you would pass. Third: If you had studied, you would have passed. Mixed: If you had studied, you would be passing now.
examples.es: Primera: Si estudias, aprobarás. Segunda: Si estudiaras, aprobarías. Tercera: Si hubieras estudiado, habrías aprobado. Mixta: Si hubieras estudiado, estarías aprobando ahora.

examples.en: I'll go if it's sunny. I'd go if I were free. I would have gone if I'd known. If I were brave, I would have tried.
examples.es: Iré si es soleado. Iría si estuviera libre. Habría ido si lo hubiera sabido. Si fuera valiente, lo habría intentado.

examples.en: Unless you change your mind, we'll leave tomorrow. If only I had known! As long as you're happy, I'm satisfied.
examples.es: A menos que cambies de opinión, nos iremos mañana. ¡Ojalá lo hubiera sabido! Siempre que seas feliz, estoy satisfecho.

days.tip: B2: Master all conditionals. They're fundamental to advanced English expression.

quiz.q: Which is third conditional? (A) If I study, I pass (B) If I studied, I'd pass (C) If I'd studied, I'd have passed
If he _____ the truth, he _____ angry now.
She _____ _____ the exam if she _____ more.

days.grammar: have/get something done, delegation of action

examples.en: I had my car repaired. She got the house painted. We had the documents translated.
examples.es: Hice reparar mi coche. Ella mandó pintar la casa. Hicimos traducir los documentos.

examples.en: They had their website designed by a professional.
examples.es: Mandaron diseñar su sitio web por un profesional.

examples.en: I'm getting my hair cut tomorrow.
examples.es: Me voy a cortar el pelo mañana.

days.tip: B2: 'Have something done' shows someone does it for you. 'Get' is more casual.

quiz.q: I _____ my teeth _____ next week.
She _____ her dress _____ because it's too long.
They _____ the new office _____ by professionals.

days.grammar: Passive in perfect forms, passive with modals, passive causative

examples.en: The report should have been submitted yesterday. The document must have been reviewed already.
examples.es: El informe debería haber sido presentado ayer. El documento ya debe haber sido revisado.

examples.en: Having been told the news, she was shocked.
examples.es: Habiendo sido informada de la noticia, estaba impactada.

examples.en: The mistake might have been made by someone else.
examples.es: El error podría haber sido cometido por alguien más.

days.tip: B2: Advanced passive forms add sophistication and precision to your expression.

flashcards.front: The mistake could have been avoided
flashcards.back: El error podría haber sido evitado

quiz.q: The letter _____ _____ _____ by now.
_____ _____ the bad news, they cancelled the trip.
The project _____ _____ delayed if we _____ started earlier.

days.grammar: Using passive for impact, tone, formality

examples.en: The city was devastated by the earthquake. Hundreds of people were injured.
examples.es: La ciudad fue devastada por el terremoto. Cientos de personas resultaron heridas.

examples.en: The vaccine was administered to millions of people. Lives were saved as a result.
examples.es: La vacuna fue administrada a millones de personas. Se salvaron vidas como resultado.

examples.en: The project was undertaken despite the risks. Despite being challenged, the team persevered.
examples.es: El proyecto fue emprendido a pesar de los riesgos. A pesar de ser desafiados, el equipo perseveró.

days.tip: B2: Passive voice in formal writing emphasizes consequences and creates appropriate tone.

quiz.q: The museum _____ _____ in 1850 and _____ _____ many times.
The victims _____ _____ to hospital and _____ _____ well.
Having _____ _____ the award, she _____ humbled.

days.grammar: Professional use of passive structures

examples.en: The suspect is believed to be armed and dangerous. It is alleged that he stole the jewels. According to witnesses, he was seen fleeing the scene.
examples.es: Se cree que el sospechoso está armado y es peligroso. Se alega que robó las joyas. Según los testigos, fue visto huyendo de la escena.

examples.en: The experiment is said to have revolutionary implications. The findings are reported to confirm the hypothesis.
examples.es: Se dice que el experimento tiene implicaciones revolucionarias. Se reporta que los hallazgos confirman la hipótesis.

days.tip: B2: News and academic English heavily feature these passive structures.

quiz.q: The new drug is _____ _____ highly effective.
The victim is _____ _____ the criminal.
It _____ _____ that the merger will be profitable.

days.grammar: All advanced passive forms mastered

examples.en: The building was designed by a famous architect. I had it renovated. It is said to be a masterpiece. Despite being damaged, it was restored.
examples.es: El edificio fue diseñado por un arquitecto famoso. Lo hice renovar. Se dice que es una obra maestra. A pesar de estar dañado, fue restaurado.

examples.en: The vaccines were administered globally. Millions had their lives saved. It is believed the pandemic has been controlled. Getting vaccinated should be a priority.
examples.es: Las vacunas fueron administradas globalmente. Millones tuvieron sus vidas salvadas. Se cree que la pandemia ha sido controlada. Vacunarse debería ser una prioridad.

days.tip: B2: These advanced structures are essential for sophisticated, professional English.

flashcards.back: have been done, should have been done
is said to, is believed to, allegedly

quiz.q: She _____ the house _____ _____ by a professional.
The project _____ _____ _____ if we'd known the risks.
It _____ _____ _____ successful. The team _____ to _____ congratulated.

days.grammar: had + past participle for events before other past events

examples.en: By the time he arrived, I had left. She had finished her work before she went out.
examples.es: Para cuando llegó, me había ido. Ella había terminado su trabajo antes de salir.

examples.en: I discovered that I had lost my keys. They had warned me, but I didn't listen.
examples.es: Descubrí que había perdido mis llaves. Me habían advertido, pero no escuché.

days.tip: B2: Past perfect clarifies which event happened first when both are in the past.

quiz.q: She _____ _____ the book before she saw the movie.
By the time we arrived, they _____ _____.
I _____ that I _____ my wallet.

days.grammar: had been + -ing for duration before a past point

examples.en: I had been waiting for two hours when he arrived. She had been studying all night before the exam.
examples.es: Había estado esperando durante dos horas cuando llegó. Ella había estado estudiando toda la noche antes del examen.

examples.en: They had been arguing for weeks before they broke up.
examples.es: Habían estado peleando durante semanas antes de separarse.

days.tip: B2: Past perfect continuous shows how long an action lasted before something else happened.

quiz.q: He _____ _____ _____ for years before he succeeded.
She _____ _____ _____ all day, so she was exhausted.
They _____ _____ _____ for weeks before I joined.

days.grammar: Combining simple past, continuous, perfect, perfect continuous

examples.en: It was a dark night. I had been walking for hours. The wind was howling. Suddenly, I heard a scream. Someone had called for help. Before I could react, a figure appeared.
examples.es: Era una noche oscura. Había estado caminando durante horas. El viento aullaba. De repente, escuché un grito. Alguien había gritado pidiendo ayuda. Antes de que pudiera reaccionar, apareció una figura.

examples.en: She had trained for months. The competition day arrived. She had been nervous. Finally, her moment came. She had performed brilliantly. The judges were impressed.
examples.es: Ella había entrenado durante meses. Llegó el día de la competencia. Había estado nerviosa. Finalmente, llegó su momento. Había actuado brillantemente. Los jueces estaban impresionados.

days.tip: B2: Use all four past tenses to tell vivid, engaging stories.

quiz.q: He _____ the document when he _____ the alarm. He _____ _____ _____ for hours.
She _____ _____ _____ for months when the opportunity _____ .
The movie _____ _____ when I _____ in. It _____ _____ for 10 minutes.

days.grammar: Multiple time layers, complex chronology

examples.en: Before he had finished college, he had already started working. While he was studying, he was also building his business. By the time he graduated, he had been running it for three years.
examples.es: Antes de que terminara la universidad, ya había comenzado a trabajar. Mientras estudiaba, también estaba construyendo su negocio. Para cuando se graduó, lo había estado dirigiendo durante tres años.

examples.en: She had been working in that company for five years. Meanwhile, she had been developing her skills. When the opportunity came, she had the experience needed.
examples.es: Había estado trabajando en esa empresa durante cinco años. Mientras tanto, había estado desarrollando sus habilidades. Cuando llegó la oportunidad, tenía la experiencia necesaria.

days.tip: B2: Manage multiple timelines simultaneously in your storytelling.

quiz.q: By the time she _____, she _____ _____ _____ for ten years.
He _____ _____ _____ for years before he _____ success.
While they _____ _____ _____ the project, I _____ another one.

days.grammar: Master chronological storytelling

examples.en: I had been living in the city for years. I was comfortable with my routine. Then one day, everything changed. A stranger had entered my life. We had met briefly before. This time was different. By the end of the day, I realized my life would never be the same.
examples.es: Había estado viviendo en la ciudad durante años. Estaba cómodo con mi rutina. Entonces un día, todo cambió. Un extraño había entrado en mi vida. Nos habíamos conocido brevemente antes. Esta vez fue diferente. Al final del día, me di cuenta de que mi vida nunca sería la misma.

examples.en: She had been dreaming of this moment since childhood. She had trained rigorously. The day finally came. As she walked onto the stage, she realized she had conquered her fears. Her performance was brilliant. The audience was amazed. By the time she finished, she had achieved something extraordinary.
examples.es: Había estado soñando con este momento desde la infancia. Había entrenado rigurosamente. El día finalmente llegó. Cuando subió al escenario, se dio cuenta de que había conquistado sus miedos. Su actuación fue brillante. La audiencia estaba asombrada. Para cuando terminó, había logrado algo extraordinario.

days.tip: B2: These narrative tenses are critical for sophisticated storytelling in English.

quiz.q: Which sentence is most natural? (A) I had been waiting for hours when he came (B) I waited for hours when he was coming (C) I have been waiting when he comes
By the time I _____, he _____ _____ _____ the house for an hour.
She _____ _____ _____ for weeks before she finally _____ the courage.

days.grammar: must have, can't have, must be, can't be for deduction

examples.en: She must have forgotten. He can't have been there.
examples.es: Debe haber olvidado. Él no pudo haber estado allí.

examples.en: This must be the answer. That can't be right.
examples.es: Esta debe ser la respuesta. Eso no puede ser correcto.

days.tip: B2: Use must/can't for 95% certainty. Should for 50-90% probability.

quiz.q: She didn't answer. She _____ _____ forgotten about the meeting.
He knows all the answers. He _____ _____ _____ studying.
This document _____ _____ fake. It's too perfect.

days.grammar: might have, could have, may have for speculation

examples.en: He might have left early. She could have been sick. They may have forgotten.
examples.es: Podría haber salido temprano. Ella podría haber estado enferma. Podrían haber olvidado.

examples.en: It might be true. This could be the solution. That may be the answer.
examples.es: Podría ser verdadero. Esta podría ser la solución. Esa podría ser la respuesta.

days.tip: B2: Might/could/may show 20-50% certainty. They're equally common.

quiz.q: I haven't seen him. He _____ _____ _____ sick.
The answer _____ _____ in this book somewhere.
They _____ _____ understood the instructions.

days.grammar: should have, ought to for what was expected to happen

examples.en: She should have arrived by now. The project should be finished. He ought to know better.
examples.es: Debería haber llegado ya. El proyecto debería estar terminado. Él debería saber mejor.

examples.en: By this time, they should have called. The weather should be nice.
examples.es: Para esta hora, deberían haber llamado. El clima debería ser agradable.

days.tip: B2: Should expresses what was expected (70-80% certainty).

quiz.q: It's 6 PM. She _____ _____ home by now.
He didn't show up. He _____ _____ _____ better.
The package _____ _____ _____ here yesterday.

days.grammar: Combining multiple modal statements for nuanced deduction

examples.en: He can't have left—his car is still here. But he might have gotten a ride. Although he should have called. This must be a misunderstanding.
examples.es: No pudo haberse ido—su auto sigue aquí. Pero podría haberse ido en un carro. Aunque debería haber llamado. Esto debe ser un malentendido.

examples.en: The burglar must have had a key. Otherwise, they couldn't have entered without breaking the window. He might have been someone familiar.
examples.es: El ladrón debe haber tenido una llave. De lo contrario, no podrían haber entrado sin romper la ventana. Podría haber sido alguien familiar.

days.tip: B2: Layer modals to show complex reasoning and argumentation.

flashcards.back: Start with must, then might, then could

quiz.q: She's not here. She _____ _____ left early. But she _____ have told us. She _____ _____ _____ traffic.
He can't _____ here yesterday because he _____ _____ _____ in Spain. So he _____ _____ then.
This _____ _____ _____ the answer. It _____ sense. He _____ _____ correct.

days.grammar: All modal deduction patterns

examples.en: She must have left early. He can't have forgotten. It might be important. They should have called. This could be serious. The answer ought to be here. She may have understood.
examples.es: Debe haber salido temprano. Él no pudo haber olvidado. Podría ser importante. Deberían haber llamado. Esto podría ser serio. La respuesta debería estar aquí. Ella podría haber entendido.

examples.en: Based on the evidence: He must have done it. She couldn't have known. They might have been involved. I should have suspected. This can't be a coincidence.
examples.es: Basado en la evidencia: Debe haberlo hecho. Ella no podría haber sabido. Ellos podrían haber estado involucrados. Debería haber sospechado. Esto no puede ser una coincidencia.

days.tip: B2: Modal mastery lets you make sophisticated deductions and arguments.

flashcards.back: must have, can't have, might have
must be, can't be, should be

options: must have done it/can't have known

quiz.q: She _____ here by now. If she isn't, she _____ _____ _____ .
This _____ _____ the solution. It _____ _____ . The answer _____ _____ _____ here.

days.grammar: Inverting subject and auxiliary for dramatic effect

examples.en: Never have I seen such beauty. Rarely does she make mistakes. Seldom will you find such quality.
examples.es: Nunca he visto tanta belleza. Rara vez comete errores. Raramente encontrarás tal calidad.

examples.en: So impressed was I that I couldn't speak. Not only did he help, but he offered advice.
examples.es: Tan impresionado estaba que no podía hablar. No solo ayudó, sino que ofreció consejo.

days.tip: B2: Inversion is literary and formal. Creates dramatic, memorable statements.

quiz.q: _____ _____ I realized the truth.
_____ _____ _____ such dedication seen.
_____ _____ the problem _____ solved.

days.grammar: It is...that, What...is, Where...is constructions

examples.en: It is the quality that matters. What she said was important. Where you go is your choice.
examples.es: Es la calidad lo que importa. Lo que ella dijo fue importante. Dónde vayas es tu elección.

examples.en: It was his honesty that impressed me. What I need is time. What she needs is support.
examples.es: Fue su honestidad lo que me impresionó. Lo que necesito es tiempo. Lo que ella necesita es apoyo.

days.tip: B2: Cleft sentences focus attention on one part of the sentence for emphasis.

quiz.q: _____ _____ _____ _____ that made the difference.
_____ _____ _____ is your determination.

days.grammar: Multiple emphasis strategies in combination

examples.en: It was not the money, but the principle that mattered. What he said was remarkable. Never before have such achievements been reached.
examples.es: No era el dinero, sino el principio lo que importaba. Lo que dijo fue notable. Nunca antes se habían alcanzado tales logros.

examples.en: Only when you understand the problem can you solve it. What is truly important is often overlooked.
examples.es: Solo cuando entiendes el problema puedes resolverlo. Lo que es verdaderamente importante a menudo se pasa por alto.

days.tip: B2: Combine emphasis techniques for sophisticated, memorable expression.

quiz.q: It _____ the destination, _____ the journey, _____ matters.
_____ _____ _____ understood the significance.
_____ I could do was wait. _____ I wanted was answers.

days.grammar: Formal emphasis techniques for impact

examples.en: It is the methodology that ensures reliability. What distinguishes this research is its scope. Never before has such comprehensive data been compiled.
examples.es: Es la metodología la que asegura la confiabilidad. Lo que distingue esta investigación es su alcance. Nunca antes se ha compilado datos tan completos.

examples.en: The key point is not the numbers, but their interpretation. What makes this study unique is its longitudinal design.
examples.es: El punto clave no son los números, sino su interpretación. Lo que hace que este estudio sea único es su diseño longitudinal.

days.tip: B2: Emphasis techniques in academic writing add sophistication and clarity.

quiz.q: _____ _____ _____ _____ that ensures success is proper planning.
_____ _____ _____ this approach _____ _____ cost-effectiveness.
_____ _____ _____ _____ been such a comprehensive review.

days.grammar: Master emphasis and inversion

examples.en: Never have I encountered such complexity. It is not what you know, but who you know that matters. What truly counts is your integrity. Not only must you be competent, but you must also be ethical.
examples.es: Nunca he encontrado tal complejidad. No es lo que sabes, sino a quién conoces lo que importa. Lo que realmente cuenta es tu integridad. No solo debes ser competente, sino que también debes ser ético.

examples.en: Only through dedication can success be achieved. It was her perseverance that made the difference. What remains essential is commitment. Rarely do we find such determination.
examples.es: Solo a través de la dedicación se puede lograr el éxito. Fue su perseverancia lo que marcó la diferencia. Lo que sigue siendo esencial es el compromiso. Raramente encontramos tal determinación.

days.tip: B2: These techniques elevate your English to sophisticated, persuasive levels.

quiz.q: Which is most emphatic? (A) I have never seen such beauty (B) Never have I seen such beauty (C) Beauty like this I have never seen
What _____ _____ is integrity. It _____ _____ that matters most.
Only _____ _____ _____ we truly understand.

days.grammar: Omitting relative pronoun + verb, using participles

examples.en: The book which I am reading is good. → The book I am reading is good.
examples.es: El libro que estoy leyendo es bueno. → El libro que estoy leyendo es bueno.

examples.en: The student who came late apologized. → The student coming late apologized.
examples.es: El estudiante que llegó tarde se disculpó. → El estudiante llegando tarde se disculpó.

days.tip: B2: Reduced clauses make speech more concise and natural.

quiz.q: The advice _____ by the expert was valuable.
The student _____ the exam passed with honors.
The document _____ today is important.

days.grammar: Nested clauses, multiple modifiers

examples.en: The book that the teacher recommended, which was on the bestseller list, became my favorite.
examples.es: El libro que el maestro recomendó, que estaba en la lista de éxitos, se convirtió en mi favorito.

examples.en: The person whom you met, whose father is famous, is my cousin.
examples.es: La persona a quien conociste, cuyo padre es famoso, es mi prima.

days.tip: B2: Layer clauses carefully for clarity and impact.

quiz.q: The project _____ we discussed, _____ seemed feasible, was approved.
The author _____ books we read, _____ lived in Paris, is my favorite.
The city _____ we visited, _____ is famous for art, captivated me.

days.topic: Relative Clauses in Academic & Professional Writing
days.grammar: Sophisticated relative structures

examples.en: The research which we have been conducting, the implications of which are significant, has been published.
examples.es: La investigación que hemos estado realizando, cuyas implicaciones son significativas, ha sido publicada.

examples.en: The methodology employed, which combines qualitative and quantitative approaches, has proven effective.
examples.es: La metodología empleada, que combina enfoques cualitativos y cuantitativos, ha demostrado ser efectiva.

days.tip: B2: Master complex relatives for sophisticated academic and professional communication.

quiz.q: The study, _____ employed 500 participants, _____ lasted six months, showed significant results.
The method _____ was used, the validity _____ has been questioned, is no longer recommended.
The initiative, _____ implementation we supervised, _____ we're proud, succeeded.

days.grammar: All relative forms, all contexts

examples.en: The person to whom I owe everything, whose help was invaluable, is my mentor.
examples.es: La persona a quien le debo todo, cuya ayuda fue invaluable, es mi mentor.

examples.en: The reason why I left, which I'll explain later, is complex. The place where I was born, which is beautiful, is far away.
examples.es: La razón por la que me fui, que explicaré más tarde, es compleja. El lugar donde nací, que es hermoso, está lejos.

days.tip: B2: Master all relative pronouns in all their forms and functions.

quiz.q: The woman _____ I spoke to, _____ name I forgot, is a doctor.
The reasons _____ she left remain unclear. The place _____ she went is unknown.
The person _____ helped me, _____ success is remarkable, is my friend.

days.grammar: Complete command of all relative structures

examples.en: The research which I conducted, the results of which are promising, has applications that are far-reaching. The colleagues whose expertise I trust, with whom I collaborate regularly, are excellent partners.
examples.es: La investigación que realicé, cuyos resultados son prometedores, tiene aplicaciones de gran alcance. Los colegas cuya experiencia confío, con quienes colaboro regularmente, son excelentes socios.

examples.en: The challenges that we faced, which were significant, taught us lessons whose value cannot be overstated. The solution we implemented, which was innovative, addresses problems that were previously overlooked.
examples.es: Los desafíos que enfrentamos, que fueron significativos, nos enseñaron lecciones cuyo valor no puede ser exagerado. La solución que implementamos, que fue innovadora, aborda problemas que fueron previamente pasados por alto.

days.tip: B2: Complex relatives show expert-level English mastery.

quiz.q: Which is most sophisticated? (A) The book that I read (B) The book which I read (C) The book I read
The study, _____ findings are published, _____ took years, reveals _____.
The person to _____ credit belongs, _____ work transformed the field, is retiring.

days.grammar: Register variation, appropriate context

examples.en: Informal: Gonna check it out later. Formal: I intend to examine it subsequently.
examples.es: Informal: Voy a revisarlo después. Formal: Tengo la intención de examinarlo posteriormente.

examples.en: Informal: He's a real jerk. Formal: His behavior demonstrates a lack of professionalism.
examples.es: Informal: Es un verdadero idiota. Formal: Su comportamiento demuestra una falta de profesionalismo.

days.tip: B2: Know both registers. Choose based on context and audience.

quiz.q: Choose formal: (A) I gotta go (B) I must depart (C) I need to leave
Rewrite informally: 'I hereby request your assistance.' → I _____ _____ _____ .
Choose for academic writing: (A) A lot of people (B) Numerous individuals (C) Many people

days.grammar: Sophisticated academic language

examples.en: The data substantiates our hypothesis. Furthermore, the implications are far-reaching. Consequently, we recommend further research.
examples.es: Los datos corroboran nuestra hipótesis. Además, las implicaciones son de gran alcance. Por consiguiente, recomendamos más investigación.

examples.en: Whereas previous studies focused on X, this research examines Y. Moreover, the methodology is innovative. Nevertheless, limitations exist.
examples.es: Mientras que estudios previos se enfocaron en X, esta investigación examina Y. Además, la metodología es innovadora. Sin embargo, existen limitaciones.

days.tip: B2: These transitions and vocabulary are essential for academic writing.

quiz.q: The findings _____ our theory. _____, further validation is needed.
_____ Smith focused on quantitative data, _____ this study employs qualitative methods.
The evidence is compelling. _____, the sample size was limited.

days.grammar: Sophisticated sentence patterns for academic writing

examples.en: It could be argued that the correlation is significant. Research indicates that further investigation is warranted. The data imply a causal relationship.
examples.es: Se podría argumentar que la correlación es significativa. La investigación indica que se justifica una investigación adicional. Los datos implican una relación causal.

examples.en: It might be contended that alternative explanations exist. The findings suggest that traditional models require revision.
examples.es: Se podría argumentar que existen explicaciones alternativas. Los hallazgos sugieren que los modelos tradicionales requieren revisión.

days.tip: B2: These phrases add academic rigor and sophistication.

quiz.q: The data _____ that traditional approaches _____ revision.
It _____ _____ that the results are inconclusive.
The findings _____ alternative explanations. Nevertheless, the primary conclusion _____ valid.

days.grammar: Blending academic and professional contexts

examples.en: Our analysis indicates that stakeholder engagement is critical. The implementation strategy addresses client concerns while maintaining strategic objectives. Consequently, we recommend a phased approach.
examples.es: Nuestro análisis indica que el compromiso de las partes interesadas es crítico. La estrategia de implementación aborda las preocupaciones del cliente mientras mantiene los objetivos estratégicos. Por consiguiente, recomendamos un enfoque por fases.

examples.en: The proposal substantiates our commitment to excellence. Moreover, the methodology aligns with industry best practices. Nevertheless, budget constraints require careful consideration.
examples.es: La propuesta corrobora nuestro compromiso con la excelencia. Además, la metodología se alinea con las mejores prácticas de la industria. Sin embargo, las limitaciones presupuestarias requieren consideración cuidadosa.

days.tip: B2: Master both academic and professional registers for diverse contexts.

quiz.q: Our _____ _____ that _____ engagement is critical for success.
The _____ strategy _____ industry _____ practices. _____, constraints exist.
Research _____ a _____ approach _____ optimal outcomes.

days.grammar: Complete command of all registers and academic language

examples.en: Formally: The circumstances necessitate further examination. Informally: We need to look into this more. Academic: Compelling evidence substantiates the hypothesis, nevertheless alternative explanations warrant consideration.
examples.es: Formalmente: Las circunstancias necesitan más examen. Informalmente: Necesitamos investigar esto más. Académicamente: La evidencia convincente corrobora la hipótesis, sin embargo las explicaciones alternativas merecen consideración.

examples.en: Professional: Our analysis indicates that implementation of best practices will optimize stakeholder value. Academic: The research suggests that the proposed methodology, while innovative, requires longitudinal validation. Informal: We think this approach will work if we stick with it.
examples.es: Profesional: Nuestro análisis indica que la implementación de mejores prácticas optimizará el valor de las partes interesadas. Académica: La investigación sugiere que la metodología propuesta, aunque innovadora, requiere validación longitudinal. Informal: Creemos que este enfoque funcionará si lo mantenemos.

days.tip: B2: Master register variation. This is a hallmark of advanced speakers.

quiz.q: Which is most academic? (A) We found some stuff (B) Data indicate findings (C) Results show something
Rewrite formally: 'We're gonna check it out.' → _____ _____ _____ the matter.
Choose professional: (A) The client wants results (B) Stakeholder value optimization is our priority (C) Customers need stuff

days.grammar: Sophisticated idioms and fixed expressions

examples.en: That's a piece of cake. He's as smart as a whip. She's up in arms about it.
examples.es: Eso es pan comido. Él es muy inteligente. Ella está furiosa al respecto.

examples.en: The ball is in your court. We're barking up the wrong tree. That's the tip of the iceberg.
examples.es: La bola está en tu cancha. Estamos ladrando en el árbol equivocado. Eso es solo la punta del iceberg.

days.tip: B2: Idiomatic English makes you sound natural and native-like.

quiz.q: The exam was a _____ _____ .
Don't worry about that; it's only the _____ _____ _____ .
The decision is _____ - the _____ _____ _____ .

days.grammar: Coherent argument structure, persuasive language

examples.en: First, I would argue that... Second, consider that... Furthermore... In conclusion, it's clear that...
examples.es: Primero, argumentaría que... Segundo, considera que... Además... En conclusión, está claro que...

examples.en: The evidence suggests that... Consequently... Despite contrary views, I maintain that... This demonstrates...
examples.es: La evidencia sugiere que... Por consiguiente... A pesar de las opiniones contrarias, mantengo que... Esto demuestra...

days.tip: B2: Strong argument structure makes your point persuasive and memorable.

quiz.q: _____, the evidence clearly _____ this point.
One _____ argue that X is true. _____, consider that Y also applies.
_____ contrary views exist, the _____ _____ _____ .

days.grammar: Expressing disagreement, defending positions

examples.en: I take issue with that claim. However, I grant that... While that's true, I would counter that... You make a valid point, but consider also...
examples.es: No estoy de acuerdo con esa afirmación. Sin embargo, reconozco que... Aunque eso es verdad, argumentaría que... Haces un punto válido, pero también considera...

examples.en: I appreciate your perspective, though I must contend that... While I concede that point, I maintain that... That's an interesting argument, but the evidence suggests otherwise.
examples.es: Aprecio tu perspectiva, aunque debo argumentar que... Aunque concedo ese punto, mantengo que... Ese es un argumento interesante, pero la evidencia sugiere lo contrario.

days.tip: B2: These phrases let you debate effectively while remaining respectful.

quiz.q: While I _____ your point, I must _____ that the evidence suggests otherwise.
I _____ issue with that claim because...
You make a valid _____, but I _____ maintain that...

days.grammar: Nuanced discussion language, acknowledgment and refinement

examples.en: To refine my position: while I acknowledge the complexities, the essential point remains... With the caveat that... To clarify my earlier assertion...
examples.es: Para refinar mi posición: aunque reconozco las complejidades, el punto esencial sigue siendo... Con la salvedad de que... Para aclarar mi afirmación anterior...

examples.en: Perhaps I should qualify that: when I say X, I mean specifically... The nuance here is important: ... To be more precise, the evidence indicates...
examples.es: Quizás debería calificar eso: cuando digo X, quiero decir específicamente... El matiz aquí es importante: ... Para ser más preciso, la evidencia indica...

days.tip: B2: These phrases show sophisticated thinking and intellectual honesty.

quiz.q: To _____ my position, I should acknowledge that...
With the _____ that exceptions exist, I maintain that...
Perhaps I should _____ my earlier point: specifically...

days.grammar: Integration of all B2 structures and strategies

examples.en: Congratulations! You have completed B2. You now command: third conditionals, advanced passives, complex relatives, formal/academic registers, persuasive argumentation, and sophisticated idioms. You can write professionally, debate effectively, and express nuanced ideas with confidence.
examples.es: ¡Felicidades! Has completado B2. Ahora dominas: terceros condicionales, pasivos avanzados, relativas complejas, registros formales/académicos, argumentación persuasiva, e idiomas sofisticados. Puedes escribir profesionalmente, debatir efectivamente, y expresar ideas matizadas con confianza.

examples.en: Your English is now advanced. You can navigate complex conversations, write academic papers, deliver professional presentations, and engage in nuanced discussions. You have achieved upper-intermediate fluency. What's next? Either continue to C1 (advanced) or perfect these B2 skills through daily practice and engagement with authentic English materials.
examples.es: Tu inglés es ahora avanzado. Puedes navegar conversaciones complejas, escribir trabajos académicos, dar presentaciones profesionales, y participar en discusiones matizadas. Has logrado fluidez de nivel intermedio superior. ¿Cuál es el siguiente paso? Continúa a C1 (avanzado) o perfecciona estas habilidades B2 a través de la práctica diaria y la participación en materiales auténticos en inglés.

days.tip: B2: You've achieved advanced fluency. You're ready for professional and academic English environments.
